to suspend or vary, or attach conditions to, any consent for the discharge of any effluent,

P2para.Text: DOE shall exercise that power in such manner as will ensure, so far as reasonably practicable, that the supplies of water available to the water undertaker are not seriously affected.
Where—

P3para.Text: any drought order confers power on DOE to suspend or vary, or attach conditions to, any consent for the discharge of any effluent; and
DOE exercises that power so as to restrict the discharge of effluent by a sewerage undertaker,

P2para.Text: the sewerage undertaker may so modify any consents or agreements relating to the discharge by other persons of trade effluent as to enable it to comply with any requirements or conditions imposed on it by or under the order with respect to discharges from sewers or works of the undertaker.
In this Article—

Para.Text: “compensation water” means water which a water undertaker or any other person is under an obligation to discharge—
in accordance with the provisions of a licence under Article 20 of the 1999 Order into any watercourse or underground strata; or
under any local statutory provision, into any river, stream or other running water or into a canal; and
“inland navigation” includes any canal or navigable river.

P1group.Title: Works under drought orders

P2para.Text: A drought order may authorise a water undertaker, subject to any conditions and restrictions specified in the order, to carry out any works required for the performance of any duty or the exercise of any power which is imposed or conferred by or under the order.
A drought order authorising a water undertaker to carry out any works—

P3para.Text: may authorise that undertaker for that purpose to enter any land specified in the order and to occupy and use the land to such extent and in such manner as may be necessary for the carrying out and maintenance of the works; and

may apply in relation to the carrying out of the works such of the provisions of Part VIII as appear to the Department to be appropriate, subject to such modifications as may be specified in the order.

P2para.Text: The Department shall include in any drought order authorising a water undertaker to enter any land provisions requiring that undertaker to give to the occupier of the land and to such other persons concerned with the land as may be specified in the order not less than 24 hours' notice of any intended entry.

Subject to paragraph (3), a drought order may make any such provision in relation to provisions of the order authorising any person to enter any land as corresponds to provision contained in Part II of Schedule 4.
Any works to be carried out under the authority of a drought order shall be included in the definition of emergency works in Article 6 of the Street Works (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (NI 19).

P1group.Title: Compensation and charges where drought order made

P2para.Text: Schedule 6 shall have effect with respect to the payment of compensation where a drought order has been made.

Except as provided by Schedule 6, neither DOE nor any water undertaker or sewerage undertaker shall incur any liability to any person for loss or damage sustained by reason of anything done in pursuance of any drought order or of any omission in pursuance of such an order.

Nothing in any drought order shall affect the right of DOE, a water undertaker or a sewerage undertaker, in the event of an interruption or diminution of the supply of water, to recover any fixed or minimum charge which might have been recovered from any person by DOE or that undertaker if there had been no such interruption or diminution.
Where a water undertaker makes an application for a drought order, the Department may recover from the water undertaker any expenses (whether of a revenue or capital nature)—

P3para.Text: in connection with any local inquiry held in respect of the application;
in the exercise of the Department's functions so far as their exercise is attributable to the application and (if the order is made) to the order,

P2para.Text: in so far as those expenses have not been recovered (whether from the water undertaker or not) under any other statutory provision.

P1group.Title: Offences against drought order

P2para.Text: If any person—

P3para.Text: takes or uses water in contravention of a prohibition or limitation imposed by or under any drought order or takes or uses water otherwise than in accordance with any condition or restriction so imposed; or
discharges water otherwise than in accordance with any condition or restriction imposed by or under a drought order,

P2para.Text: he shall be guilty of an offence.
If any person—

P3para.Text: fails to construct or maintain in good order a gauge, weir or other apparatus for measuring the flow of water which he was required to construct or maintain by any drought order; or

fails to allow some person authorised for the purpose by or under a drought order to inspect and examine any such apparatus or any records made thereby or kept by that person in connection therewith or to take copies of any such records,

P2para.Text: he shall be guilty of an offence.

In any proceedings against any person for an offence under this Article it shall be a defence for that person to show that he took all reasonable precautions and exercised all due diligence to avoid the commission of the offence.
A person who is guilty of an offence under this Article shall be liable—

P3para.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding the statutory maximum;
on conviction on indictment, to a fine.

P1group.Title: Interpretation of this Chapter

P1para.Text: In this Chapter—

P3para.Text: references to the taking of water include references to the collection, impounding, diversion or appropriation of water; and

references to an obligation or to a restriction include references to an obligation or, as the case may be, to a restriction which is imposed by or under any statutory provision (including this Order) or any agreement.

Chapter.Title: PROMOTION OF THE EFFICIENT USE OF WATER

P1group.Title: Duty to promote efficient use of water

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of every water undertaker to promote the efficient use of water by its customers.
The duty of a water undertaker under this Article shall be enforceable under Article 30—

P3para.Text: by the Department; or
with the consent of or in accordance with a general authorisation given by the Department, by the Authority.

P2para.Text: Nothing in this Part shall have effect to authorise or require a water undertaker to impose any requirement on any of its customers or potential customers.

P1group.Title: Power of the Authority to impose requirements on water undertakers

P2para.Text: The Authority may require a water undertaker, in its performance of its duty under Article 145, to—

P3para.Text: take any such action; or
achieve any such overall standards of performance,

P2para.Text: as the Authority may specify in the document imposing the requirement.

Where the Authority, in the document imposing a requirement on a water undertaker under paragraph (1), stipulates that any contravention of the requirement by the undertaker will be a breach of its duty under Article 145, any contravention of that requirement by the undertaker shall be a breach of that duty.
Without prejudice to the generality of paragraph (1), a requirement under that paragraph may—

P3para.Text: require a water undertaker to make available to its customers or potential customers such facilities as may be specified in the document imposing the requirement;

require a water undertaker to provide or make available to its customers or potential customers such information as may be specified in the document imposing the requirement, and may specify the form in which, the times at which or the frequency with which any such information is to be provided or made available.

P2para.Text: In exercising its powers under this Article in relation to any water undertaker, the Authority shall have regard to the extent to which water resources are available to that undertaker.
Before imposing any requirement on a water undertaker under paragraph (1) the Authority shall consult that undertaker.

Nothing in this Article authorises the Authority to impose any requirement on a water undertaker which has or may have the effect of authorising or requiring that undertaker to impose any requirement on any of its customers or potential customers.

P1group.Title: Publicity of requirements imposed under Article 146

P2para.Text: Where, under Article 146(1), the Authority imposes any requirement on a water undertaker, the Authority may arrange for that requirement to be publicised in any such manner as the Authority may consider appropriate for the purpose of bringing it to the attention of that undertaker's customers.
Without prejudice to the generality of paragraph (1), the Authority may arrange for such publicising of the requirement as is mentioned in that paragraph by—

P3para.Text: itself publicising the requirement or causing it to be publicised; or
directing the undertaker to inform or arrange to inform its customers of the requirement.

P1group.Title: Information as to compliance with requirements under Article 146

P2para.Text: Where a water undertaker is subject to any requirement imposed under Article 146(1), the Authority may arrange for there to be given to the customers of that undertaker at such times or with such frequency, and in any such manner, as the Authority may consider appropriate, such information about the level of performance achieved by the undertaker in relation to that requirement as appears to the Authority to be expedient to be given to those customers.
Without prejudice to the generality of paragraph (1), the Authority may arrange for such giving of information as is mentioned in that paragraph by—

P3para.Text: itself disseminating the information or causing it to be disseminated; or
directing the undertaker to give or arrange to give the information to its customers.

P2para.Text: At such times and in such form or manner as the Authority may direct, a water undertaker shall provide the Authority with such information as may be specified in the direction in connection with the undertaker's performance in relation to any requirement imposed upon the undertaker under Article 146(1).

A water undertaker who fails without reasonable excuse to do anything required of him by paragraph (3) shall be guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 5 on the standard scale.

Title.Emphasis: Principal duties and standards of performance

P1group.Title: General duty to provide sewerage system

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of every sewerage undertaker—

P3para.Text: to provide, improve and extend such a system of public sewers (whether inside its area or elsewhere) and so to cleanse and maintain those sewers and any lateral drains which belong to or vest in the undertaker as to ensure that that area is and continues to be effectually drained; and

to make provision for the emptying of those sewers and such further provision (whether inside its area or elsewhere) as is necessary from time to time for effectually dealing, by means of waste water treatment works or otherwise, with the contents of those sewers.

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of a sewerage undertaker in performing its duty under paragraph (1) to have regard—

P3para.Text: to its existing and likely future obligations to allow for the discharge of trade effluent into its public sewers; and
to the need to provide for the disposal of trade effluent which is so discharged.

P2para.Text: The duty of a sewerage undertaker under paragraph (1) shall be enforceable under Article 30—

P3para.Text: by the Department; or
with the consent of or in accordance with a general authorisation given by the Department, by the Authority.

P2para.Text: The obligations imposed on a sewerage undertaker by the following Chapters of this Part, and the remedies available in respect of contraventions of those obligations, shall be in addition to any duty imposed or remedy available by virtue of any provision of this Article or Article 150 and shall not be in any way qualified by any such provision.

In this Article “trade effluent” has the same meaning as in Chapter III of this Part; and, accordingly, Article 198 shall have effect for the purposes of this Article as it has effect for the purposes of Chapter III of this Part.

P1group.Title: Standards of performance in connection with provision of sewerage services

P2para.Text: For the purpose—

P3para.Text: of facilitating the determination of the extent to which breaches of the obligations imposed by virtue of the following provisions of this Part are to amount to breaches of the duty imposed by Article 149; or
of supplementing that duty by establishing overall standards of performance in relation to the provision of sewerage services by any sewerage undertaker,

P2para.Text: the Department may, in accordance with Article 152, by regulations provide for contraventions of such requirements as may be prescribed to be treated for the purposes of this Order as breaches of that duty.

The Department may, in accordance with Article 152, by regulations prescribe such standards of performance in connection with the provision of sewerage services as, in its opinion, ought to be achieved in individual cases.

Regulations under paragraph (2) may provide that, if a sewerage undertaker fails to meet a prescribed standard, it shall pay such amount as may be prescribed to any person who is affected by the failure and is of a prescribed description.
Without prejudice to the generality of the power conferred by paragraph (2), regulations under that paragraph may—

P3para.Text: include in a standard of performance a requirement for a sewerage undertaker, in prescribed circumstances, to inform a person of his rights by virtue of any such regulations;
provide for any dispute under the regulations to be referred by either party to the dispute to the Authority;
make provision for the procedure to be followed in connection with any such reference and for the Authority's determination on such a reference to be enforceable in such manner as may be prescribed;
prescribe circumstances in which a sewerage undertaker is to be exempted from requirements of the regulations.

P2para.Text: Where the Authority determines any dispute in accordance with regulations under this Article it shall, in such manner as may be specified in the regulations, give its reasons for reaching its decision with respect to the dispute.

P1group.Title: Information with respect to levels of performance

P2para.Text: The Authority shall from time to time collect information with respect to—

P3para.Text: the compensation paid by sewerage undertakers under regulations under Article 150(2); and
the levels of overall performance achieved by sewerage undertakers in connection with the provision of sewerage services.

P2para.Text: At such times as the Authority may direct, each sewerage undertaker shall give the following information to the Authority—

P3para.Text: as respects each standard prescribed by regulations under Article 150(2), the number of cases in which compensation was paid and the aggregate amount or value of that compensation; and
as respects each standard established by regulations under Article 150(1)(b), such information with respect to the level of performance achieved by the undertaker as may be prescribed.

P2para.Text: A sewerage undertaker which without reasonable excuse fails to do anything required of it by paragraph (2) shall be liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 5 on the standard scale.

The Authority shall, at least once in every year, arrange for the publication, in such form and in such manner as it considers appropriate, of such of the information collected by or given to the Authority under this Article as it may appear to the Authority expedient to give to customers or potential customers of sewerage undertakers.
In arranging for the publication of any such information the Authority shall have regard to the need for excluding, so far as practicable—

P3para.Text: any matter which relates to the affairs of an individual, where publication of that matter would or might, in the opinion of the Authority, seriously and prejudicially affect the interests of that individual; and

any matter which relates specifically to the affairs of a particular body of persons, whether corporate or not, where publication of that matter would or might, in the opinion of the Authority, seriously and prejudicially affect the interests of that body.

P1group.Title: Procedure for regulations under Article 150

P2para.Text: The Department shall not make any regulations under Article 150 unless—

P3para.Text: the Authority has made to the Department a written application complying with paragraph (3);
the Department is satisfied that a copy of the application has been served by the Authority on—

P4para.Text: every sewerage undertaker specified in the application; and
persons or bodies appearing to the Department to be representative of persons likely to be affected by the regulations;

P3para.Text: such period as the Department considers appropriate has been allowed for the making—

P4para.Text: by the Authority; or
by any affected sewerage undertaker or person or body on whom a copy of the application has been served under sub-paragraph (b)(ii),

P3para.Text: of representations or objections with respect to the Authority's proposals and any modifications proposed by the Department; and

the Department has considered the summary mentioned in paragraph (3)(c), the Authority's reasons for its proposals and every representation or objection which has been duly made with respect to those proposals, or any proposed modifications of those proposals, and has not been withdrawn.

P2para.Text: Before making an application to the Department under this Article the Authority shall arrange for such research as it considers appropriate with a view to discovering the views of a representative sample of persons likely to be affected and consider the results.
An application made by the Authority to the Department complies with this paragraph if it—

P3para.Text: sets out the draft provisions proposed by the Authority for inclusion in regulations under Article 150;
specifies the sewerage undertaker or undertakers in relation to which it is proposed those provisions should apply;
is accompanied by a written summary of the results of the research carried out in accordance with paragraph (2); and
summarises the Authority's reasons for its proposals.

P2para.Text: The Department shall not make any regulations under Article 150 except where—

P3para.Text: the only provisions of the regulations are those proposed by the Authority in its application or those provisions with such modifications as the Department considers appropriate; and
each of the modifications (if any) of the Authority's proposals to which effect is given by the regulations is a modification the proposal to make which has been notified—

P4para.Text: to the Authority; and
to any sewerage undertaker appearing to the Department to be likely to be affected by the modifications.

P1group.Title: Information to be given to customers about overall performance

P2para.Text: Each sewerage undertaker shall, in such form and manner and with such frequency as the Authority may direct, take steps to inform its customers of—

P3para.Text: the standards of overall performance established under Article 150(1)(b) which are applicable to that undertaker; and
that undertaker's level of performance as respects each of those standards.

P2para.Text: In giving any such direction, the Authority shall not specify a frequency of less than once in every period of 12 months.
The duty of a sewerage undertaker to comply with this Article shall be enforceable by the Authority under Article 30.

Title.Emphasis: Requisition of public sewer or lateral drain

P1group.Title: Duty to comply with sewer and lateral drain requisition

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of a sewerage undertaker (in accordance with Article 156) to provide a public sewer to be used for the drainage for domestic purposes of premises in a particular locality in its area if—

P3para.Text: the undertaker is required to provide the sewer by a notice served on the undertaker by the owner or occupier of any premises in that locality;
the premises in that locality the drainage of which would be by means of that sewer are—

P4para.Text: premises on which there are buildings; or
premises on which there will be buildings when proposals made by any person for the erection of any buildings are carried out; and

P3para.Text: the conditions specified in Article 155 are satisfied in relation to that requirement.

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of a sewerage undertaker (in accordance with Article 156) to provide a lateral drain to communicate with a public sewer and to be used for the drainage for domestic purposes of premises in its area if—

P3para.Text: the undertaker is required to provide the lateral drain by a notice served on the undertaker by the owner or occupier of premises the drainage of which would be by means of that lateral drain;
the premises the drainage of which would be by means of that lateral drain are—

P4para.Text: premises on which there are buildings; or
premises on which there will be buildings when proposals made by any person for the erection of any buildings are carried out; and

P3para.Text: the conditions specified in Article 155 are satisfied in relation to that requirement.

P2para.Text: The duty of a sewerage undertaker under this Article to provide a public sewer or a lateral drain shall be owed to the person who requires the provision of the sewer or lateral drain or, as the case may be, to each of the persons who joins in doing so.

Where a duty is owed by virtue of paragraph (3) to any person, any breach of that duty which causes that person to sustain loss or damage shall be actionable at the suit of that person; but, in any proceedings brought against a sewerage undertaker in pursuance of this paragraph, it shall be a defence for the undertaker to show that it took all reasonable steps and exercised all due diligence to avoid the breach.
In this Article the reference to domestic purposes, in relation to the drainage of premises to which a requirement under this Article relates, is a reference—

P3para.Text: where there are buildings on those premises, to such domestic sewerage purposes as are specified in relation to those buildings in the requirement; and

where any person is proposing to erect buildings on those premises, to such domestic sewerage purposes as are so specified in relation to the buildings and to times after the erection of the buildings.

P1group.Title: Financial conditions of compliance

P2para.Text: The conditions mentioned in Article 154(1)(c) and (2)(c) are satisfied in relation to a requirement for the provision of a public sewer or, as the case may be, lateral drain by a sewerage undertaker if—

P3para.Text: such undertakings as the undertaker may have reasonably required in accordance with paragraph (2) have been given by the person or persons who have required the provision of the sewer or lateral drain; and

such security as the undertaker may have reasonably required has been provided for the discharge of any obligations imposed by those undertakings on any person who, under paragraph (3) , may be required to secure his undertakings.

P2para.Text: The undertakings which a sewerage undertaker may require for the purposes of paragraph (1) in respect of any public sewer or lateral drain are undertakings which—

P3para.Text: bind the person or persons mentioned in that paragraph to pay to the undertaker the reasonable costs of providing that sewer or lateral drain, as determined in accordance with the undertaker's charges scheme; and
in the case of undertakings binding two or more persons, bind them either jointly and severally or with liability to pay apportioned in such manner as they may agree.

P2para.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (1)(b) a person may be required to secure his undertakings in relation to the provision of a public sewer or, as the case may be, lateral drain if he is not a public authority.

Where for the purposes of paragraph (1)(b) any sums have been deposited with a sewerage undertaker by way of security for the discharge of any obligation, the undertaker shall pay interest at such rate as may be determined either—

P3para.Text: by the undertaker with the approval of the Authority; or
in default of a determination under sub-paragraph (a), by the Authority,

P2para.Text: on every sum so deposited for every three months during which it remains in the hands of the undertaker.
An approval or determination given or made by the Authority for the purposes of paragraph (4)—

P3para.Text: may be given or made in relation to the provision of a particular public sewer or lateral drain, in relation to the provision of public sewers or lateral drains of a particular description or in relation to the provision of public sewers or lateral drains generally; and
may be revoked at any time.

P2para.Text: Any dispute between a sewerage undertaker and any other person as to—

P3para.Text: the undertakings or security required by the undertaker for the purposes of this Article; or
the amount required to be paid in pursuance of any such undertaking,

P2para.Text: may be referred to the Authority for determination under Article 61 by either party to the dispute.

P1group.Title: Determination of completion date and route for requisitioned sewer or lateral drain

P2para.Text: A sewerage undertaker shall not be in breach of a duty imposed by Article 154 in relation to any locality or (in the case of a lateral drain) in relation to any premises unless—

P3para.Text: the period of 6 months beginning with the relevant day has expired; and
the sewerage undertaker has not, before the end of that period, so laid (as the case may be)—

P4para.Text: the public sewer to be provided as to enable drains and private sewers to be used for the drainage of premises in the locality to communicate with the public sewer; or
the lateral drain to be provided as to enable the drain to be used for the drainage of premises to communicate with a public sewer vested in that undertaker,

P3para.Text: at the place or places determined under paragraph (3).

P2para.Text: The period mentioned in paragraph (1)(a) may be extended—

P3para.Text: by agreement between the undertaker and the person or persons who required the provision of the public sewer or, as the case may be, lateral drain; or
where there is a dispute as to whether the period should be extended, by the Authority on a reference under paragraph (4).

P2para.Text: The places mentioned in paragraph (1)(b) shall be—

P3para.Text: such place or places as are determined by agreement between the sewerage undertaker and the person or persons who required the provision of the public sewer or, as the case may be, lateral drain; or
in default of agreement, such place or places as are determined by the Authority on a reference under paragraph (4) to be the place or places at which it is reasonable, in all the circumstances—

P4para.Text: in relation to the provision of a public sewer, for drains or private sewers to be used for the drainage of premises in the locality in question to communicate with the public sewer; or

in relation to the provision of a lateral drain, for the lateral drain to communicate with a public sewer vested in the undertaker and for the remainder of the drain of which the lateral drain forms part to connect with the lateral drain.

P2para.Text: A reference for the purposes of paragraph (2) or (3) may be made to the Authority for determination under Article 61 by either party to the dispute.

In this Article “relevant day”, in relation to a requirement to provide a public sewer for any locality or, as the case may be, a lateral drain, means the day after whichever is the later of the following—

P3para.Text: the day on which the conditions specified in Article 155 are satisfied in relation to the requirement; and
the day on which the place or places where (as the case may be)—

P4para.Text: drains or private sewers to be used for the drainage of premises in that locality will communicate with the public sewer; or
the lateral drain will communicate with a public sewer and the remainder of the drain will connect with the lateral drain,

P3para.Text: are determined under paragraph (3).

Title.Emphasis: Provision of sewers other than by requisition

P1group.Title: Further duty to provide sewers

P2para.Text: Without prejudice to Article 154, it shall be the duty of a sewerage undertaker to provide a public sewer to be used for the drainage for domestic sewerage purposes of premises in a particular locality in its area if the conditions specified in paragraph (2) are satisfied.
The conditions mentioned in paragraph (1) are—

P3para.Text: that the premises in question, or any of those premises, are premises on which there are buildings;

that the drains or sewers used for the drainage for domestic sewerage purposes of the premises in question do not, either directly or through an intermediate drain or sewer, connect with a public sewer; and

that the drainage of any of the premises in question is giving, or is likely to give, rise to such adverse effects to the environment or amenity that it is appropriate, having regard to any guidance issued under this Article by the Department and all other relevant considerations, to provide a public sewer for the drainage for domestic purposes of the premises in question.

P2para.Text: Without prejudice to the generality of paragraph (2)(c), regard shall be had to the following considerations, so far as relevant, in determining whether it is appropriate for any sewer to be provided by virtue of this Article—

P3para.Text: the geology of the locality in question or of any other locality;
the number of premises, being premises on which there are buildings, which might reasonably be expected to be drained by means of that sewer;
the costs of providing that sewer;

the nature and extent of any adverse effects to the environment or amenity arising, or likely to arise, as a result of the premises, or, as the case may be, the locality in question not being drained by means of a public sewer; and

the extent to which it is practicable for those effects to be overcome otherwise than by the provision (whether by virtue of this Article or otherwise) of public sewers, and the costs of so overcoming those effects.

P2para.Text: Guidance issued by the Department under this Article may—

P3para.Text: relate to how regard is to be had to the considerations mentioned in sub-paragraphs (a) to (e) of paragraph (3);
relate to any other matter which it considers may be a relevant consideration in any case and to how regard is to be had to any such matter;

set out considerations, other than those mentioned in sub-paragraphs (a) to (e) of paragraph (3), to which (so far as relevant) regard shall be had in determining whether it is appropriate for any sewer to be provided by virtue of this Article;
relate to how regard is to be had to any such consideration as is mentioned in sub-paragraph (c); and
without prejudice to sub-paragraphs (a) to (d), relate to how a sewerage undertaker is to discharge its functions under this Article.

P2para.Text: Before issuing guidance under this Article the Department shall consult—

P3para.Text: DOE;
the Authority;
sewerage undertakers; and
such other bodies or persons as it considers appropriate;

P2para.Text: and the Department shall arrange for any guidance issued by it under this Article to be published in such manner as it considers appropriate.
Subject to the following provisions of this Article, the duty of a sewerage undertaker by virtue of paragraph (1) shall be enforceable under Article 30—

P3para.Text: by the Department; or
with the consent of or in accordance with a general authorisation given by the Department, by the Authority.

P2para.Text: Any dispute between a sewerage undertaker and an owner or occupier of any premises in its area as to—

P3para.Text: whether the undertaker is under a duty by virtue of paragraph (1) to provide a public sewer to be used for any such drainage of those premises as is mentioned in that paragraph;
the domestic sewerage purposes for which any such sewer should be provided; or
the time by which any such duty of the undertaker should be performed,

P2para.Text: shall be determined by the Authority, and may be referred to the Authority for determination by either of the parties to the dispute.
The Authority—

P3para.Text: shall notify the parties of the reasons for its decision on any dispute referred to it under paragraph (7); and
may make any such recommendation, or give any such guidance, relating to or in connection with the drainage of the premises or locality in question as it considers appropriate.

P2para.Text: The decision of the Authority on any dispute referred to it under paragraph (7) shall be final.

A sewerage undertaker shall only be taken to be in breach of its duty under paragraph (1) where, and to the extent that, it has accepted, or the Authority has determined under this Article, that it is under such a duty and where any time accepted by it, or determined by the Authority under this Article, as the time by which the duty is to that extent to be performed has passed.

P1group.Title: Power to construct lateral drains following provision of public sewer

P2para.Text: Where a sewerage undertaker provides a public sewer pursuant to a duty to do so imposed on it by Article 154 or Article 157, the undertaker may, at the request of the person mentioned in paragraph (2), also provide at the same time one or more lateral drains to be used for the drainage for domestic sewerage purposes of premises in its area and to communicate with that sewer.
A request under paragraph (1) may be made—

P3para.Text: in the case of a public sewer to be provided under Article 154, by the person who requires the provision of the sewer under that Article; and

in the case of a public sewer to be provided under Article 157, by the owner or occupier of any premises in respect of which the duty to provide the sewer arises under that Article (but any request may only be for the provision of a lateral drain to his premises).

P2para.Text: The person making a request under this Article shall pay to the sewerage undertaker, following provision of the lateral drain, the costs reasonably incurred in or in connection with providing that drain.
Any dispute between the sewerage undertaker and the person making a request under this Article as to—

P3para.Text: whether a lateral drain should be provided pursuant to the request; or
the costs reasonably incurred in the provision of a lateral drain,

P2para.Text: may be referred to the Authority for determination under Article 61 by either party to the dispute.
Any lateral drain provided pursuant to a request made to a sewerage undertaker under this Article shall belong to the undertaker.

Title.Emphasis: Adoption of sewers , sustainable drainage systems and waste water treatment works

P1group.Title: Adoption of sewers , sustainable drainage systems and waste water treatment works

P2para.Text: Subject to the following provisions of this Article and to Articles 160, 162 and 206(3), a sewerage undertaker may at any time declare that—

P3para.Text: any sewer which is situated within its area or which serves the whole or any part of that area; or
any lateral drain which communicates or is to communicate with a public sewer which—

P4para.Text: is so situated or serves the whole or any part of that area; and
is vested in that undertaker; or

Text.Addition: any sustainable drainage system which is so situated or which serves the whole or any part of that area; or

P3para.Text: any waste water treatment works which are so situated or which serve the whole or any part of that area,

P2para.Text: shall, as from such date as may be specified in the declaration, become vested in the undertaker.

The owner, or any of the owners, of any sewer, lateral drain , sustainable drainage system or waste water treatment works with respect to which a sewerage undertaker might make a declaration under this Article may make an application to that undertaker requesting it to make a declaration under this Article with respect to the sewer, lateral drain , system or works.
A declaration or application under this Article may be made with respect to a part only of a sewer.
A sewerage undertaker which proposes to make a declaration under this Article—

P3para.Text: shall give notice of its proposal to the owner or owners of the sewer, lateral drain , system or works in question; and

shall take no further action in the matter until two months have elapsed without an appeal against the proposal being lodged under Article 162 or, as the case may be, until any appeal so lodged has been determined.

P2para.Text: A sewerage undertaker, in deciding whether a declaration should be made under this Article, shall have regard to all the circumstances of the case and, in particular, to the following considerations, that is to say—

P3para.Text: whether the sewer , system or works in question is or are adapted to, or required for, any general system of sewerage or sewage disposal which the undertaker has provided, or proposes to provide, for the whole or any part of its area;
whether the sewer , lateral drain or system is constructed under a road or under land reserved by a planning scheme for a street;

the number of buildings which the sewer , lateral drain or system is intended to serve, and whether, regard being had to the proximity of other buildings or the prospect of future development, it is likely to be required to serve additional buildings;
the method of construction and state of repair of the sewer, lateral drain , system or works; and
in a case where an owner objects, whether the making of the proposed declaration would be seriously detrimental to him.

P2para.Text: Any person who immediately before the making of a declaration under this Article was entitled to use the sewer , lateral drain or system in question shall be entitled to use it, or any sewer , lateral drain or system substituted for it, to the same extent as if the declaration had not been made.
No declaration may be made under this Article in respect of—

P3para.Text: any sewer or works the construction of which was completed before 1st October 1973;
any lateral drain the construction of which was completed before the transfer date.

P1group.Title: Adoption under Article 159: supplementary

P2para.Text: Where a sewerage undertaker is about to take into consideration the question of making a declaration under Article 159 with respect to—

P3para.Text: any sewer which is situated within the area of another sewerage undertaker or which, though situated within its own area, serves the whole or any part of the area of another sewerage undertaker;

any lateral drain which is situated within the area of another sewerage undertaker or which, though situated within its own area, communicates or is to communicate with a public sewer which is situated within or serves the whole or any part of the area of another sewerage undertaker;...

Text.Addition: any sustainable drainage system which is situated within the area of another sewerage undertaker or which, though situated in its own area, serves the whole or any part of the area of another sewerage undertaker; or

P3para.Text: any waste water treatment works which are situated within the area of another sewerage undertaker or which, though situated within its own area, serve the whole or any part of the area of another sewerage undertaker,

P2para.Text: it shall give notice to the other undertaker.
Where a sewerage undertaker is required to give notice under paragraph (1) to another undertaker, no declaration under Article 159 shall be made by the former undertaker until either—

P3para.Text: the other undertaker has consented to the declaration; or
the Department, on an application made to it, has dispensed with the necessity for such consent, either unconditionally or subject to such conditions as it may consider fit to impose.

P2para.Text: Where—

P3para.Text: a sewer (or part of a sewer) , a lateral drain or a sustainable drainage system is vested, or any waste water treatment works are vested, in a relevant body; and

in the case of a sewer, part of a sewer, lateral drain , sustainable drainage system or works vested in a railway undertaking or harbour authority, the sewer, part , lateral drain or system in question is, or the works are, situated in or on land belonging to that operator or authority and held or used by that operator or authority for the purposes of its undertaking,

P2para.Text: a sewerage undertaker shall not make a declaration under Article 159 with respect to (as the case may be) the sewer, or part of it, or the lateral drain or the system or the works, except on the application of the relevant body concerned.
Where a sewerage undertaker makes a declaration under Article 159 with respect to—

P3para.Text: a sewer , lateral drain or sustainable drainage system which is situated within the area of another sewerage undertaker; or
any waste water treatment works which are so situated,

P2para.Text: it shall forthwith give notice of the fact to that other undertaker.
In this Article “relevant body” means any sewerage undertaker, district council, railway undertaking or harbour authority.

P1group.Title: Agreements to adopt sewer, drain , sustainable drainage system or waste water treatment works at future date

P2para.Text: Subject to paragraph (7) and Article 206(3), a sewerage undertaker may agree with—

P3para.Text: any person constructing or proposing to construct—

P4para.Text: any sewer;
any drain which is intended to communicate with a public sewer vested in that undertaker;...

Text.Addition: any sustainable drainage system; or

P4para.Text: any waste water treatment works; or

P3para.Text: any person at whose expense the undertaker is, by virtue of an agreement under Article 221, to carry out work in connection with the construction of such a drain or sewer,

P2para.Text: that, if the sewer, drain , sustainable drainage system or waste water treatment works is or are constructed in accordance with the terms of the agreement, the undertaker will, upon completion of the work, at some specified date or on the happening of some future event, declare the sewer or such part of the drain as constitutes the lateral drain or the system or the works (as the case may be) to be vested in that undertaker.
A person mentioned in sub-paragraph (a) or (b) of paragraph (1) may make an application to a sewerage undertaker requesting the undertaker to make an agreement under this Article.

An application under paragraph (2) shall be accompanied and supplemented by all such information as the undertaker may reasonably require; but, subject to paragraph (4) and without prejudice to the effect (if any) of any other contravention of the requirements of this Article in relation to such an application, a failure to provide information in pursuance of the obligation to supplement such an application shall not invalidate the application.
Where—

P3para.Text: a person who has made an application to a sewerage undertaker under paragraph (2) has failed to comply with his obligation under this Article to supplement that application with information required by the undertaker; and

that requirement was made by the undertaker at such a time before the end of the period within which the undertaker is required, by virtue of Article 162, to respond to the application as gave that person a reasonable opportunity to provide the required information within that period,

P2para.Text: the undertaker may delay its response to the application until a reasonable time after the required information is provided.

Any agreement made under this Article by a sewerage undertaker shall be enforceable against the undertaker by the owner or occupier for the time being of any premises served by the sewer, lateral drain , system or works to which it relates.
Without limiting the terms which may be included in an agreement under this Article—

P3para.Text: such terms may include terms as to the provision of such security as the undertaker may reasonably require for the discharge of any obligations imposed on the other party to the agreement; ...
such terms relating to a drain may include in particular —

P4para.Text: identification of that part of the drain which constitutes the lateral drain for the purposes of the agreement and, in particular, the point or points of connection between that part and the remainder of the drain;
a requirement for the installation of an inspection chamber, at the expense of the person with whom the sewerage undertaker is to make the agreement, at a place specified in the agreement;

provision, if the inspection chamber is constructed in accordance with the terms of the agreement, for the undertaker to declare that the inspection chamber be vested in the undertaker at the same time as the lateral drain; and
provision for the lateral drain, once vested in the undertaker, to communicate with a public sewer at the place or places specified in the agreement; and

Text.Addition: an agreement relating to a sewer or drain may include terms requiring the other party to construct one or more sustainable drainage systems designed to receive surface water from any premises served by that sewer or drain.
In paragraph (6)(c) “
” means the person with whom the sewerage undertaker is to make the agreement.

P2para.Text: A sewerage undertaker shall not make an agreement under this Article with respect to—

P3para.Text: a sewer, drain , sustainable drainage system or waste water treatment works situated within the area of another sewerage undertaker; or
a drain which is intended to communicate with a sewer which—

P4para.Text: is so situated; or
is vested in another sewerage undertaker,

P3para.Text: until one of the conditions mentioned in paragraph (8) is satisfied.

P2para.Text: The conditions are—

P3para.Text: that the other undertaker has consented to the making of the agreement; or
that the Department, on an application made to it, has dispensed with the necessity for such consent, either unconditionally or subject to such conditions as it may consider fit to impose.

Text.Addition: The Department may issue guidance (to any person) about applying for, and making, agreements under this Article; and sewerage undertakers must have regard to the guidance.
Before issuing guidance under this Article the Department must consult—
the Authority;
DOE;
sewerage undertakers;
the General Consumer Council for Northern Ireland;
such other persons as the Department considers it appropriate to consult in relation to the guidance.

P1group.Title: Appeals with respect to adoption

P2para.Text: An owner of any sewer, lateral drain , sustainable drainage system or waste water treatment works may appeal to the Authority if—

P3para.Text: he is aggrieved by the proposal of a sewerage undertaker to make a declaration under Article 159; or
he is aggrieved by the refusal of a sewerage undertaker to make such a declaration.

Text.Substitution: A person who has entered into or wants to enter into an agreement under Article 161 may appeal to the Authority about any matter concerning the agreement (including whether it is concluded, its terms and its operation).

P2para.Text: The time for the making of an appeal under paragraph (1) by the owner of any sewer, lateral drain , sustainable drainage system or waste water treatment works shall be—

P3para.Text: in the case of an appeal by virtue of sub-paragraph (a) of that paragraph, any time within two months after notice of the proposal is served on that owner; and

in the case of an appeal by virtue of sub-paragraph (b) of that paragraph, any time after receipt of notice of the undertaker's refusal or, if no such notice is given, at any time after the end of two months from the making of the application for the declaration.

P2para.Text: On the hearing of an appeal under this Article, the Authority may—

P3para.Text: in the case of an appeal under paragraph (1), allow or disallow the proposal of the sewerage undertaker or, as the case may be, make any declaration which the sewerage undertaker might have made; or
in the case of an appeal under paragraph (2)—

P4para.Text: uphold the refusal of the undertaker to grant the application or to modify the terms offered; or
on behalf of the undertaker, refuse the application or enter into any agreement into which the undertaker might have entered on the application;

P3para.Text: and any declaration made under sub-paragraph (a) shall have the same effect as if it had been made by the undertaker in question.

P2para.Text: Where the Authority makes a declaration under paragraph (4)(a), it may, if it thinks fit—

P3para.Text: specify conditions, including conditions as to the payment of compensation by the sewerage undertaker; and
direct that its declaration shall not take effect unless any conditions so specified are accepted.

P2para.Text: Where the Authority makes an agreement under paragraph (4)(b) on behalf of a sewerage undertaker, it may do so on such terms as it considers reasonable or, as the case may be, on the terms offered by the undertaker subject to such modifications as the Authority considers appropriate for ensuring that the terms of the agreement are reasonable.

The Authority, in deciding on an appeal under this Article whether any declaration or agreement should be made, shall have regard to all the circumstances of the case and, in particular, to the considerations specified in Article 159(5); and for the purposes of this paragraph, in its application in relation to an appeal under paragraph (2), sub-paragraphs (a) to (e) of Article 159(5) shall have effect with the necessary modifications.

Title.Emphasis: Communication of drains and private sewers with public sewers

P1group.Title: Right to communicate with public sewers

P2para.Text: Subject to the provisions of this Article and Article 163A —

P3para.Text: the owner or occupier of any premises, or
the owner of any private sewer which drains premises,

P2para.Text: shall be entitled to have his drains or sewer communicate with the public sewer of any sewerage undertaker and thereby to discharge foul water and surface water from those premises or that private sewer.
In this Article, and in Articles 164 to 166, 168, 170 to 173, 175, 176, 182, 185, 198 and 206—

P3para.Text: references (however expressed) to a public sewer include a public lateral drain which satisfies sewer standards; and
for the purposes of sub-paragraph (a)—

P4para.Text: a “public lateral drain” is a lateral drain which either belongs to the sewerage undertaker or is vested in the sewerage undertaker by virtue of a declaration made under Article 159 or under an agreement made under Article 161; and
“sewer standards” means such standards of construction and repair as the undertaker would require if the public lateral drain or part of it were to become a public sewer.

P2para.Text: Subject to the provisions of Chapter III of this Part, nothing in paragraph (1) shall entitle any person—

P3para.Text: to discharge directly or indirectly into any public sewer—

P4para.Text: any liquid from a factory, other than domestic sewage or surface or storm water, or any liquid from a manufacturing process; or
any liquid or other matter the discharge of which into public sewers is prohibited by or under any statutory provision; or

P3para.Text: where separate public sewers are provided for foul water and for surface water, to discharge directly or indirectly—

P4para.Text: foul water into a sewer provided for surface water; or
except with the approval of the undertaker, surface water into a sewer provided for foul water; or

P3para.Text: to have his drains or sewer made to communicate directly with a storm-water overflow sewer.

P2para.Text: A person whose private sewer or drains do not communicate with a public sewer and who is desirous of availing himself of his entitlement under this Article shall give notice of his proposals to the sewerage undertaker in question.

At any time within 21 days after a sewerage undertaker receives a notice under paragraph (4), the undertaker may by notice to the person who gave the notice refuse to permit the communication to be made, if it appears to the undertaker that either of the following grounds is satisfied.

Text.Addition: The first ground is that the mode of construction or condition of the drain or sewer, or of any associated sustainable drainage system—
does not satisfy the standards reasonably required by the undertaker; or
is such that the making of the communication would be prejudicial to the undertaker's sewerage system.

The second ground, in the case of a drain or sewer discharging surface water into the public sewer, is that suitable alternatives exist, or could reasonably be provided, for the discharge of that water from the premises or sewer in question.
The reference in paragraph (5B) to suitable alternatives includes, in particular—
the use of landscaping, natural features or any other kind of arrangement, or
the design or construction of any other feature (whether or not amounting to a “structure” within the meaning of Article 2(3A)),
for the purpose of reducing the volume of water from the premises or sewer that enters public sewers or watercourses, or the rate at which it does so.

P2para.Text: For the purpose of examining the mode of construction and condition of a drain or sewer to which a notice under paragraph (4) relates , or of an associated sustainable drainage system, a sewerage undertaker may, if necessary, require it to be laid open for inspection.

Where the sewer or drain , or the system, satisfies the standards reasonably required by it, a sewerage undertaker may, as a condition of permitting the communication to be made, require that the sewer or that part of the drain forming the lateral drain , or the system, be vested in it by virtue of a declaration under Article 159.
Any question arising under paragraphs (4) to (7) between a sewerage undertaker and a person proposing to make a communication as to—

P3para.Text: the reasonableness of the undertaker's refusal to permit a communication to be made; or
as to the reasonableness of any requirement under paragraph (6) or (7),

P2para.Text: may, on the application of that person, be determined by the Authority under Article 61 and, accordingly, Article 162 shall not apply to any requirement under paragraph (7).
In this Article “factory” has the same meaning as in the Factories Act (Northern Ireland) 1965 (c. 20).

Text.Addition: In this Article an “associated sustainable drainage system” is any sustainable drainage system from which water is or is to be discharged, whether directly or indirectly, into a drain or sewer to which a notice under paragraph (4) relates.

Title.Addition: Communication as of right only where adoption agreement entered into

Text.Addition: This Article applies to a person's entitlement under Article 163(1) to have a sewer communicate with a public sewer.
The person may exercise that entitlement only if an agreement complying with Conditions 1, 2 and 3 was entered into under Article 161 in respect of the sewer.
Condition 1 is that the agreement included provision about the standards according to which the following were to be constructed—
the sewer in question,
any drain which communicates or is intended to communicate, whether directly or indirectly, with that sewer and was constructed at the same time as it,
any associated sustainable drainage system, and
any associated waste water works.
Condition 2 is that the agreement included provision about the adoption of the following by the sewerage undertaker—
the sewer in question,
such part of any drain mentioned in paragraph (3)(b) as constitutes a lateral drain,
any associated sustainable drainage system, and
any associated waste water works.

Condition 3 is that the agreement included terms such as are described in Article 161(6)(a) (provision of security for the discharge of obligations imposed on the party other than the sewerage undertaker).

If an agreement has been entered into in respect of a sewer and all the obligations imposed on the other party to the agreement have been complied with, the undertaker may not refuse to permit the communication in reliance on Article 163(5).
In this Article—
an “associated sustainable drainage system” is any sustainable drainage system—
constructed at the same time as the sewer in question, and
from which water is or is to be discharged, whether directly or indirectly, into that sewer;
“associated waste water works” are any waste water treatment works—
constructed at the same time as the sewer in question, and
from which effluent is or is to be discharged, whether directly or indirectly, into that sewer; and
the “other party to the agreement” is the person mentioned in sub-paragraph (a) or (b) of Article 161(1).

P1group.Title: Right of sewerage undertaker to undertake the making of communications with public sewers

P2para.Text: Where a person gives to a sewerage undertaker notice under Article 163 of his proposal to have his drains or sewer made to communicate with a public sewer of that undertaker, the undertaker may—

P3para.Text: within 21 days after the receipt of the notice; or
if any question arising under the notice requires to be determined by the Authority, within 21 days after the determination of that question,

P2para.Text: give notice to that person that the undertaker intends itself to make the communication.

If, after a notice has been given to any person under paragraph (1), that person proceeds himself to make the communication, he shall be guilty of an offence and liable, on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding level 4 on the standard scale.
Where a sewerage undertaker has given a notice under paragraph (1)—

P3para.Text: the undertaker shall have all such rights in respect of the making of the communication as the person desiring it to be made would have; but
it shall not be obligatory on the undertaker to make the communication until either—

P4para.Text: there has been paid to the sewerage undertaker any such sum, not exceeding the undertaker's reasonable estimate of the cost of the work, as the undertaker may have required to be paid to it; or
there has been given to the undertaker such security for the payment of the cost of the work as it may reasonably have required.

P2para.Text: If any payment made to a sewerage undertaker under paragraph (3) exceeds the expenses reasonably incurred by it in the carrying out of the work in question, the excess shall be repaid by the undertaker; and, if and so far as those expenses are not covered by such a payment, the undertaker may recover summarily as a civil debt the expenses, or the balance of them, from the person for whom the work was done.
Any dispute between a sewerage undertaker and any other person as to—

P3para.Text: whether the undertaker's estimate of the cost of works given under paragraph (3)(b)(i) is reasonable,
whether any requirement of security for the payment of the cost of works was reasonably made by the undertaker, or
whether any excess is repayable, or any expenses are recoverable, by the undertaker under paragraph (4), or the amount of any such excess or expenses,

P2para.Text: may be referred to the Authority for determination under Article 61 by either party to the dispute.
For the purposes of this Article, the making of the communication between a drain or private sewer and a public sewer includes all such work as involves the breaking open of a street.

P1group.Title: Communication works by person entitled to communication

P2para.Text: Where a sewerage undertaker does not under Article 164 elect itself to make a communication to which a person is entitled under Article 163, the person making it shall—

P3para.Text: before commencing the work, give reasonable notice to any person directed by the undertaker to superintend the carrying out of the work; and
afford any such person all reasonable facilities for superintending the carrying out of the work.

P2para.Text: For the purpose—

P3para.Text: of exercising his rights under Article 163; or
of examining, repairing or renewing any drain or private sewer draining his premises into a public sewer,

P2para.Text: the owner or occupier of any premises shall be entitled to exercise the same powers as, for the purpose of carrying out its functions, are conferred on a sewerage undertaker by Articles 219 and 222(1).

The provisions of Part VIII of this Order shall apply, with the necessary modifications, in relation to the power conferred by paragraph (2) as they apply in relation to the power conferred by Articles 219 and 222(1).

P1group.Title: Unlawful communications

P2para.Text: Any person who causes a drain or sewer to communicate with a public sewer—

P3para.Text: in contravention of any of the provisions of Article 163 or 165; or
before the end of the period mentioned in paragraph (5) of Article 163,

P2para.Text: shall be guilty of an offence and liable, on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding level 4 on the standard scale.
Whether proceedings have or have not been taken by a sewerage undertaker in respect of an offence under this Article, such an undertaker may—

P3para.Text: close any communication made in contravention of any of the provisions of Article 163 or 165; and
recover summarily as a civil debt from the offender any expenses reasonably incurred by the undertaker in so doing.

P1group.Title: New connections with public sewers

P2para.Text: Where, on the application of any qualifying person—

P3para.Text: it appears to the Authority that it is necessary or expedient for the purposes of this Part that the sewerage undertaker specified in the application (“the established undertaker”) should permit a main connection into its sewerage system and
the Authority is satisfied that the making of such a connection cannot be secured by agreement,

P2para.Text: the Authority may by order require the established undertaker to allow the connection for such period and on such terms and conditions as may be provided in the order.
In this Article “qualifying person” means—

P3para.Text: a sewerage undertaker; or
a person who has made an application for an appointment or variation under Article 15 which has not been determined.

P2para.Text: In paragraph (1) a “main connection” means a connection—

P3para.Text: between a sewer or disposal main and a sewer or disposal main; or
a connection which allows a sewer or disposal main to discharge directly into a waste water treatment works.

P2para.Text: Where the application is made by a person who is not a sewerage undertaker at the time when the application is made, an order made under this Article in response to that application shall be expressed not to come into force until the applicant becomes a sewerage undertaker for the area specified in the order, or for an area which includes that area.

Subject to paragraph (4), an order under this Article shall have effect as an agreement between the established undertaker and the applicant but may be varied or revoked by a subsequent order made by the Authority on the application of either party to the agreement, as well as by agreement between the parties.
The Authority shall not make an order under this Article unless it has first consulted the Department.
In exercising its functions under this Article, the Authority shall have regard to the desirability of—

P3para.Text: facilitating effective competition within the sewerage services industry;
the existing undertaker's recovering the expenses of complying with its obligations by virtue of this Article and securing a reasonable return on its capital;

the existing undertaker's being able to meet its existing obligations, and likely future obligations, to provide sewerage services without having to incur unreasonable expenditure in carrying out works;
not putting at risk the ability of the existing undertaker to meet its existing obligations, or likely future obligations, to provide such services.

P1group.Title: Restrictions on use of public sewers

P2para.Text: Subject to the provisions of Chapter III of this Part, no person shall throw, empty or turn, or suffer or permit to be thrown or emptied or to pass, into any public sewer, or into any drain or sewer communicating with a public sewer or into a sustainable drainage system or into any waste water treatment works—

P3para.Text: any matter likely to—

P4para.Text: injure the sewer, drain , system or works,
interfere with the free flow of its contents or
affect prejudicially the treatment and disposal of its contents,

P3para.Text: either alone or in combination with the contents of the sewer, drain , system or works; or
any such chemical refuse or waste steam, or any such liquid of a temperature higher than 43 degrees Celsius, as is by virtue of paragraph (2) a prohibited substance; or
any petroleum spirit or carbide of calcium.

P2para.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (1), chemical refuse, waste steam or a liquid of a temperature higher than that mentioned in that paragraph is a prohibited substance if (either alone or in combination with the contents of the sewer, drain , system or works in question) it is or, in the case of the liquid, is when so heated—

P3para.Text: dangerous;
the cause of a nuisance; or
injurious, or likely to cause injury, to health.

P2para.Text: A person who contravenes any of the provisions of this Article shall be guilty of an offence and liable—

P3para.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding the statutory maximum and to a further fine not exceeding £50 for each day on which the offence continues after conviction;
on conviction on indictment, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or to a fine or to both.

P2para.Text: For the purposes of so much of paragraph (3) as makes provision for the imposition of a daily penalty—

P3para.Text: the court by which a person is convicted of the original offence may fix a reasonable date from the date of conviction for compliance by the defendant with any directions given by the court; and
where a court has fixed such a period, the daily penalty shall not be imposed in respect of any day before the end of that period.

P2para.Text: In this Article the expression “petroleum spirit” means any such—

P3para.Text: crude petroleum;
oil made from petroleum or from coal, shale, peat or other bituminous substances; or
product of petroleum or mixture containing petroleum,

P2para.Text: as, when tested in the manner prescribed by or under the Petroleum (Consolidation) Act (Northern Ireland) 1929 (c. 13), gives off an inflammable vapour at a temperature of less than 23 degrees Celsius.

P1group.Title: Requirement that proposed drain , sewer or sustainable drainage system be constructed so as to form part of general system

P2para.Text: Where—

P3para.Text: a person proposes to construct a drain , sewer or sustainable drainage system ; and

a sewerage undertaker considers that the proposed drain , sewer or system is, or is likely to be, needed to form part of a general sewerage system which that undertaker provides or proposes to provide,

P2para.Text: the undertaker may require that person to construct the drain , sewer or system in a manner differing, as regards material or size of pipes, depth, fall, direction or outfall or otherwise, from the manner in which that person proposes, or could otherwise be required by the undertaker, to construct it.
If any person on whom requirements are imposed under this Article by a sewerage undertaker is aggrieved by the requirements, he may within 28 days appeal to the Authority.
On an appeal under paragraph (2) with respect to any requirements, the Authority may either disallow the requirements or allow them with or without modification.
It shall be the duty of a person on whom requirements are imposed by a sewerage undertaker under this Article to comply with those requirements.

The duty of any person by virtue of paragraph (4) to comply with the requirements of a sewerage undertaker shall be owed to the undertaker; and any breach of that duty which causes the undertaker to sustain loss or damage shall be actionable at the suit of the undertaker.
A sewerage undertaker which exercises the powers conferred on it by this Article shall—

P3para.Text: repay to the person constructing the drain , sewer or system the extra expenses reasonably incurred by that person in complying with the undertaker's requirements; and

until the drain or sewer becomes a public sewer, , or the system is vested in the undertaker by virtue of a declaration under Article 159, from time to time repay to that person so much of any expenses reasonably incurred by him in repairing or maintaining the drain , sewer or system as may be attributable to the undertaker's requirements having been imposed and complied with.

P2para.Text: Nothing in this Article shall apply in relation to so much of any drain , sewer or sustainable drainage system as is proposed to be constructed by any railway undertaking or harbour authority in or on land which—

P3para.Text: belongs to that undertaking or authority; and
is held or used by it for the purposes of its undertaking.

Text.Addition: A requirement imposed under this Article may not be inconsistent with, or more onerous than, standards agreed in an agreement under Article 161.

P1group.Title: Power to alter drainage system of premises in area

P2para.Text: Where any premises have a drain or sewer communicating with a public sewer or a cesspool, but that system of drainage, though sufficient for the effectual drainage of the premises—

P3para.Text: is not adapted to the general sewerage system of the area; or
is, in the opinion of the sewerage undertaker for the area, otherwise objectionable,

P2para.Text: the undertaker may, at its own expense, close the existing drain or sewer and fill up the cesspool, if any, and do any work necessary for that purpose.

The power conferred on a sewerage undertaker by paragraph (1) shall be exercisable on condition only that the undertaker first provides, in a position equally convenient to the owner of the premises in question, a drain or sewer which—

P3para.Text: is equally effectual for the drainage of the premises; and
communicates with a public sewer.

P2para.Text: A sewerage undertaker which proposes to carry out any work under this Article shall give notice of its proposals to the owner of the premises in question.

If the owner of the premises is aggrieved by the proposals, whether as regards the position or the sufficiency of the drain or sewer proposed to be provided for the drainage of the premises, he may refer the matter to the Authority for determination under Article 61.

The Department may by regulations make provision with respect to consents and the conditions of consents for discharges of trade effluent into the sewer of a sewerage undertaker through a drain or sewer provided in pursuance of this Article.
In this Article—

Para.Text: “cesspool” includes a settlement tank, septic tank or other tank for the reception or disposal of foul matter from buildings; and
“trade effluent” has the same meaning as in Chapter III.

P1group.Title: Power to investigate defective drain , sewer or sustainable drainage system

P2para.Text: Where it appears to a sewerage undertaker that there are reasonable grounds for believing—

P3para.Text: that any drain connecting with a public sewer, or any private sewer so connecting, or any sustainable drainage system from which water is discharged, whether directly or indirectly, into any such drain or private sewer, is in such a condition as to be injurious or likely to cause injury to health or as to be a nuisance; or
that any such drain , private sewer or system is so defective as to admit subsoil water,

P2para.Text: the undertaker may examine the condition of the drain , sewer or system and, for that purpose, may apply any test, other than a test by water under pressure and, if the undertaker deems it necessary, open the ground.

If on examination the drain , sewer or system is found to be in proper condition, the undertaker shall, as soon as possible, reinstate any ground which has been opened by it and make good any damage done by the undertaker.

Title.Emphasis: Use of pipes for sewerage purposes

P1group.Title: Use of road drains as sewers and vice versa

P2para.Text: Subject to the provisions of this Article, the Department and a sewerage undertaker may agree that—

P3para.Text: any drain or sewer which is vested in the Department for the purposes of its functions under the Roads (Northern Ireland) Order 1993 (NI 15) may, upon such terms as may be agreed, be used by the undertaker for the purpose of conveying surface water from premises or roads;
any public sewer vested in the undertaker may, upon such terms as may be agreed, be used by the Department for conveying surface water from roads.

P2para.Text: Where a sewer or drain with respect to which the Department and a sewerage undertaker propose to make an agreement under this Article discharges, whether directly or indirectly, into the sewers or waste water treatment works of another sewerage undertaker, the agreement shall not be made without the consent of that other undertaker.
Subject to paragraph (4), a consent given by a sewerage undertaker for the purposes of paragraph (2) may be given on such terms as that undertaker thinks fit.
Neither the Department nor a sewerage undertaker shall—

P3para.Text: unreasonably refuse to enter into an agreement for the purposes of this Article; or
insist unreasonably upon terms unacceptable to the other party;

P2para.Text: and a sewerage undertaker shall not unreasonably refuse to consent to the making of such an agreement or insist unreasonably upon terms unacceptable to either party.
Any question arising under this Article as to whether or not the Department or any sewerage undertaker is acting unreasonably may be referred by either party to the Appeals Commission.
Nothing in this Article affects the powers of the Department under Article 45 of the Roads (Northern Ireland) Order 1993 (NI 15) (road drains).

P1group.Title: Power to close or restrict use of public sewer

P2para.Text: Subject to paragraph (3), a sewerage undertaker may discontinue and prohibit the use of any public sewer which is vested in the undertaker.
A discontinuance or prohibition under this Article may be for all purposes, for the purpose of foul water drainage or for the purpose of surface water drainage.
Before any person who is lawfully using a sewer for any purpose is deprived under this Article by a sewerage undertaker of the use of the sewer for that purpose, the undertaker shall—

P3para.Text: provide a sewer which is equally effective for his use for that purpose; and
at the undertaker's own expense, carry out any work necessary to make that person's drains or sewers communicate with the sewer provided in pursuance of this paragraph.

P2para.Text: Any dispute arising under paragraph (3)(a) between a sewerage undertaker and any other person as to the effectiveness of any sewer provided by the undertaker for that person's use may be referred to the Authority for determination under Article 61 by either party to the dispute.

P1group.Title: Interpretation of Chapter II

P2para.Text: In this Chapter “domestic sewerage purposes”, in relation to any premises, means any one or more of the following purposes, that is to say—

P3para.Text: the removal, from buildings on the premises and from land occupied with and appurtenant to the buildings, of the contents of lavatories;
the removal, from such buildings and from such land, of water which has been used for cooking or washing; and
the removal, from such buildings and such land, of surface water;

P2para.Text: but does not, by virtue of sub-paragraph (b), include the removal of any water used for the business of a laundry or for a business of preparing food or drink for consumption otherwise than on the premises.

References in this Chapter to the construction of a sewer , of a sustainable drainage system or of any waste water treatment works include references to the extension of any existing sewer , system or works.
Every application made or consent given under this Chapter shall be made or given in writing.

Nothing in Articles 159 to 166 or in Articles 168 to 173 shall be construed as authorising a sewerage undertaker to construct or use any public or other sewer, or any drain or outfall , or any sustainable drainage system —

P3para.Text: in contravention of any applicable provision of the Water Order; or

for the purpose of conveying foul water into any natural or artificial watercourse, pond or lake, without the water having been so treated as not to affect prejudicially the purity and quality of the water in that watercourse, pond or lake.

P2para.Text: A sewerage undertaker shall so carry out its functions under Articles 159 to 162, 169, 172 and 173 as not to create a nuisance.

Title.Emphasis: Consent for discharge of trade effluent into public sewer

P1group.Title: Consent required for discharge of trade effluent into public sewer

P2para.Text: Subject to the following provisions of this Chapter, the occupier of any trade premises in the area of a sewerage undertaker may discharge any trade effluent proceeding from those premises into the undertaker's public sewers if he does so with the undertaker's consent.
Nothing in this Chapter shall authorise the discharge of any effluent into a public sewer otherwise than by means of a drain or sewer.
The following, that is to say—

P3para.Text: the restrictions imposed by sub-paragraphs (a) and (b) of Article 163(3); and
Article 168 so far as it relates to anything falling within sub-paragraph (a) or (b) of paragraph (1) of that Article,

P2para.Text: shall not apply to any discharge of trade effluent which is lawfully made by virtue of this Chapter.

Accordingly, paragraphs (4) to (9) of Article 163 and Articles 165 and 166 shall have effect in relation to communication with a sewer for the purpose of making any discharge which is lawfully made by virtue of this Chapter as they have effect in relation to communication with a sewer for the purpose of making discharges which are authorised by paragraph (1) of Article 163.

If, in the case of any trade premises, any trade effluent is discharged without such consent or other authorisation as is necessary for the purposes of this Chapter, the occupier of the premises shall be guilty of an offence and liable—

P3para.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding the statutory maximum and to a further fine not exceeding £50 for each day on which the offence continues after conviction; and
on conviction on indictment, to a fine.

P1group.Title: Application for consent
