Nothing in this Article shall require a relevant undertaker to alter or remove any pipe , system or apparatus which is kept installed in, under or over any street.

A relevant undertaker may make it a condition of complying with the duty to which it is subject by virtue of a notice served by any person under paragraph (1) that such security as the undertaker may reasonably require has been provided for the discharge of any obligation of that person under paragraph (5).

Where a relevant undertaker carries out any works under this Article by virtue of a notice having been served by any person under paragraph (1), the undertaker shall be entitled to recover any expenses reasonably incurred in carrying out those works from that person.

Where any sums have been deposited with a relevant undertaker by way of security for the discharge of any obligation under paragraph (5), the undertaker shall pay interest at such rate as may be determined either—

P3para.Text: by the undertaker with the approval of the Authority; or
in default of a determination under sub-paragraph (a), by the Authority.

P2para.Text: on every sum so deposited for every 3 months during which it remains in the hands of the undertaker.

An approval or determination by the Authority for the purposes of paragraph (6) may be given or made in relation to a particular case or description of cases or generally and may be revoked at any time.
The duty of a relevant undertaker under this Article shall be enforceable under Article 30 by the Authority.
In this Article—

Para.Text: “improvement”, in relation to any land, includes any development or change of use but does not include an improvement with respect to the supply of water, or the provision of sewerage services, to any premises; and
“relevant pipe” has the same meaning as in Article 219.

P1group.Title: Protective provisions in respect of watercourses etc.

P2para.Text: Nothing in the relevant sewerage provisions shall authorise a sewerage undertaker injuriously to affect—

P3para.Text: any reservoir, canal, watercourse, river or stream, or any feeder thereof; or
the supply, quality or fall of water contained in, or in any feeder of, any reservoir, canal, watercourse, river or stream,

P2para.Text: without the consent of any person who would, apart from this Order, have been entitled by law to prevent, or be relieved against, the injurious affection of, or of the supply, quality or fall of water contained in, that reservoir, canal, watercourse, river, stream or feeder.

Any dispute as to whether the supply, quality or fall of water in any reservoir, canal, watercourse, river, stream or feeder is injuriously affected by the exercise of powers under the relevant sewerage provisions shall be referred, at the option of the party complaining, to the arbitration of a single arbitrator to be appointed by agreement between the parties or, in default of agreement, by the President of the Institution of Civil Engineers.

P1group.Title: Works in tidal lands etc.

P2para.Text: Nothing in any of the provisions of this Part relating to any relevant works power shall authorise any relevant undertaker to carry out any works at any place below the place to which the tide flows at mean high water springs, except in accordance with such plans and sections, and subject to such restrictions, as may, before the works are commenced, have been approved by the Secretary of State and the Crown Estate Commissioners.
An approval for the purposes of paragraph (1) shall be given to a relevant undertaker by the service on that undertaker of a notice containing the approval.

In paragraph (1) the reference to a relevant works power is a reference to a power conferred by any of the relevant sewerage provisions or by any of Articles 219, 220, 222, 224 and 226, except the power conferred by Article 222(3).

P1group.Title: Power to sell materials deriving from works

P2para.Text: A relevant undertaker may sell any materials which—

P3para.Text: have been removed by that undertaker from any premises, including any street, when carrying out works under, or otherwise carrying into effect any provision of this Order; and
are not before the end of 3 days from the date of their removal claimed by the owner and taken away by him.

P2para.Text: Where a relevant undertaker sells any materials under this Article, it shall pay the proceeds to the person to whom the materials belonged after deducting the amount of any expenses incurred by the undertaker.
This Article—

P3para.Text: does not apply to refuse removed by a relevant undertaker; and

is not to be taken as prejudicing the determination of the rights and liabilities of a relevant undertaker when exercising a power in any case to which the preceding provisions of this Article do not apply.

P1group.Title: Rights as to sewage

P1para.Text: A sewerage undertaker—

P3para.Text: shall be entitled to all sewage discharged into its sewers or waste water treatment works; and
may process, sell or otherwise dispose of such sewage.

P1group.Title: Interpretation of Part VIII

P2para.Text: In this Part “discharge pipe” means a pipe from which discharges are or are to be made under Article 226.

In this Part references to maintaining a pipe include references to cleansing it and references to altering a pipe include references to altering its size or course, to moving or removing it and to replacing it with a pipe which is of the same description of relevant pipe (within the meaning of Article 219) as the pipe replaced.
The powers conferred by this Part on a relevant undertaker shall be exercisable both inside and outside the undertaker's area.

A relevant undertaker proposing to exercise any of its powers under Article 219 or 220 outside its own area shall, if paragraph (5) applies, give notice of its proposal to the water undertaker or (as the case may be) sewerage undertaker for the area in question and, if that paragraph applies, shall not carry out its proposal—

P3para.Text: without the consent of that other undertaker; or

where that other undertaker refuses to give its consent, or fails to give its consent before the end of the period of 28 days beginning with the day on which it is notified of the proposal, without the consent of the Authority.

P2para.Text: This paragraph applies where the proposal is to lay—

P3para.Text: a water main which is not intended to be—

P4para.Text: a trunk main; or
a water main used solely for the purpose of supplying water otherwise than for domestic purposes; or

P3para.Text: a sewer which is intended to be a public sewer but not a storm-water overflow sewer.

P2para.Text: In so far as any powers conferred by this Part on a relevant undertaker authorise the removal of any pipe or the alteration of its size or course, those powers shall be subject to such obligations by virtue of which the undertaker is required—

P3para.Text: to maintain a pipe or a connection with it; or
to alter a pipe only where certain conditions are satisfied,

P2para.Text: as are imposed on the undertaker by or under any statutory provision.
The powers conferred by virtue of this Part are without prejudice to any power conferred by virtue of any agreement and are cumulative.

P1group.Title: The Authority's register

P2para.Text: The Authority shall, at such premises and in such form as it may determine, maintain a register for the purposes of Part III and of Article 201.
Subject to any direction given under paragraph (3), the Authority shall cause to be entered in the register the provisions of—

P3para.Text: every appointment under Chapter I of Part III, every termination or transfer of any such appointment, every variation of the area for which any company holds any such appointment and every modification of the conditions of any such appointment;
every direction, consent or determination given or made under any such appointment by the Department, the CMA or the Authority;
every final enforcement order made under Article 30, every provisional enforcement order made or confirmed under that Article and every revocation of such a final or provisional enforcement order;
every undertaking given to and accepted by the Department or the Authority for the purposes of paragraph (1)(b) of Article 31 and every notice under paragraph (3) of that Article;
every penalty imposed under Article 35(1) or (2) and every notice under Article 35(5); and
every special administration order and every discharge of such an order.

P2para.Text: If it appears to the Department that the entry of any provision in the register would be against the public interest, it may direct the Authority not to enter that provision in the register; and the Authority shall comply with any such direction.
The Authority shall also cause to be entered on the register the provisions of any guidance given to it by the Department under Article 201(8).
It shall be the duty of the Authority to secure that the contents of the register are available, at all reasonable times, for inspection by the public free of charge.

It shall be the duty of the Authority, on the payment of such sum as may be reasonable, to provide a person who requests it with a copy of, or of an extract from, the contents of any part of the register.
Any sums received by the Authority under this Article shall be paid into the Consolidated Fund.

P1group.Title: Reasons for decisions

P2para.Text: This Article applies to the following decisions of the Authority or the Department—

P3para.Text: the modification of the conditions of an appointment under Chapter I of Part III or the variation of the area to which an appointment relates;
the termination of such an appointment;
the giving of any directions or consent in pursuance of a condition included in such an appointment by virtue of Article 19(1);
the determination of a question referred in pursuance of a condition included in such an appointment by virtue of Article 19(2); and
the making of a final enforcement order, the making or confirmation of a provisional enforcement order or the revocation of a final order or of a provisional order which has been confirmed.

P2para.Text: As soon as reasonably practicable after making such a decision the Authority or the Department shall publish a notice stating the reasons for the decision in such manner as it considers appropriate for the purpose of bringing the matters to which the notice relates to the attention of persons likely to be interested.
A body publishing a notice under paragraph (2) shall serve a copy on the company holding the appointment to which the decision relates.

A body preparing a notice under paragraph (2) shall have regard to the need for excluding, so far as that is practicable, any matter which relates to the affairs of a particular individual or body of persons (corporate or unincorporate), where it considers that publication of that matter would or might seriously and prejudicially affect the interests of that individual or body.

This Article does not apply in relation to a decision of the Authority resulting in any provision which the Authority was directed under Article 253(3) not to enter in the register required to be kept under that Article.

P1group.Title: Trade effluent registers

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of every sewerage undertaker to secure that copies of—

P3para.Text: every consent given by the undertaker under Chapter III of Part VI;
every direction given or having effect as if given by the undertaker under that Chapter;
every joint notice given to the undertaker under Article 177(5)(a);
every agreement entered into or having effect as if entered into by the undertaker under Article 187; and
every notice served on the undertaker under Article 190,

P2para.Text: are kept available, at all reasonable times, for inspection by the public free of charge at the offices of the undertaker.

It shall be the duty of every sewerage undertaker, on the payment of such sum as may be reasonable, to provide a person who requests it with a copy of, or of an extract from, anything kept available for inspection under this Article.
The duties of a sewerage undertaker under this Article shall be enforceable under Article 30 by the Authority.

P1group.Title: Register for the purposes of works discharges

P2para.Text: Every water undertaker shall keep a register of persons and premises for the purposes of Article 227.

A water undertaker shall enter the name and address of a person in that register in respect of any premises which abut on any watercourse if that person has requested to be so registered and is either—

P3para.Text: the owner or occupier of those premises; or
an officer of an association of owners or occupiers of premises which abut on that watercourse and include those premises.

P2para.Text: If any water undertaker contravenes, without reasonable excuse, any of the requirements of this Article, it shall be guilty of an offence and liable, on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding level 3 on the standard scale.

P1group.Title: Maps of waterworks

P2para.Text: Subject to paragraphs (6) and (7), it shall be the duty of every water undertaker to keep records of the location of—

P3para.Text: every resource main, water main or discharge pipe which is for the time being vested in that undertaker; and
any other underground works, other than a service pipe, which are for the time being vested in that undertaker.

P2para.Text: Subject to paragraph (6), it shall also be the duty of every water undertaker to keep records of the location and other relevant particulars of—

P3para.Text: every water main in relation to which a declaration of vesting has been made by the undertaker under Chapter II of Part IV but has not taken effect; and
every water main which is the subject of any agreement to make such a declaration which has been entered into by (or on behalf of) the undertaker.

P2para.Text: For the purposes of this Article the other relevant particulars of a water main are (in addition to its location) particulars of whether it is a water main in relation to which a declaration has been made under Chapter II of Part IV or a water main which is the subject of an agreement to make such a declaration.

It shall be the duty of every water undertaker to secure that the contents of any records for the time being kept by it under this Article are available, at all reasonable times, for inspection by the public free of charge at an office of the undertaker.
Any information which is required under this Article to be made available by a water undertaker for inspection by the public shall be so made available in the form of a map.

For the purpose of determining whether any failure to make a modification of any records kept under this Article constitutes a breach of the duty imposed by paragraph (1) or (2), that duty shall be taken to require any modification of the records to be made as soon as reasonably practicable after the completion of the works which make the modification necessary; and, where records kept under this Article are modified, the date of the modification and of the completion of the works making the modification necessary shall be incorporated in the records.
Nothing in this Article shall require a water undertaker, at any time before the tenth anniversary of the transfer date, to keep records of—

P3para.Text: any pipe which was laid before the transfer date; or
any underground works which were completed before the transfer date,

P2para.Text: unless those particulars were shown on the day before the transfer date on a map kept by the Department under Article 48 of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1973 (NI 2) (maps of underground works).
The duties of a water undertaker under this Article shall be enforceable under Article 30 by the Department.
In this Article “discharge pipe” has the same meaning as in Part VIII.

P1group.Title: Sewer maps

P2para.Text: Subject to paragraphs (5) to (7), it shall be the duty of every sewerage undertaker to keep records of the location and other relevant particulars—

P3para.Text: of every public sewer, lateral drain or disposal main which is vested in the undertaker;
of every sewer or lateral drain in relation to which a declaration of vesting has been made by the undertaker under Chapter II of Part VI but has not taken effect; and
of every drain or sewer which is the subject of any agreement to make such a declaration which has been entered into by the undertaker under Article 161 .

P2para.Text: For the purposes of this Article the relevant particulars of a drain, sewer or disposal main are (in addition to its location) particulars—

P3para.Text: of whether it is a drain, sewer or disposal main and of the descriptions of effluent for the conveyance of which it is or is to be used; and

of whether it is vested in the undertaker or, if it is not, of whether it is a sewer in relation to which a declaration has been made under Chapter II of Part VI or a drain or sewer which is the subject of an agreement under Article 161.

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of every sewerage undertaker to secure that the contents of all the records for the time being kept by it under this Article are available, at all reasonable times, for inspection by the public free of charge at an office of the undertaker.
Any information which is required under this Article to be made available by a sewerage undertaker for inspection by the public shall be so made available in the form of a map.

For the purpose of determining whether any failure to make a modification of any records kept under this Article constitutes a breach of the duty imposed by paragraph (1), that duty shall be taken to require any modification of the records to be made as soon as reasonably practicable after the completion of the works which make the modification necessary; and, where records kept under this Article are modified, the date of the modification and of the completion of the works making the modification necessary shall be incorporated in the records.
Nothing in this Article shall require a sewerage undertaker to keep records of any particulars of—

P3para.Text: a lateral drain laid before the transfer date; or
any other drain, or any sewer or disposal main laid before the transfer date if—

P4para.Text: the undertaker does not know of, or have reasonable grounds for suspecting, the existence of the drain, sewer or disposal main; or
it is not reasonably practicable for the undertaker to discover the course of the drain, sewer or disposal main and it has not done so.

P2para.Text: Nothing in this Article shall require a sewerage undertaker, at any time before the tenth anniversary of the transfer date, to keep records of any particulars of any such drain, sewer or disposal main laid before the transfer date as would not be excluded from its records by virtue of paragraph (6)(b) unless—

P3para.Text: those particulars were shown on the day before the transfer date on a map kept by the Department under Article 48 of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1973 (NI 2) (sewer maps); or
it is a drain or sewer in relation to which a declaration of vesting, or an agreement to make such a declaration, has been made since the transfer date.

P2para.Text: The duties of a sewerage undertaker under this Article shall be enforceable under Article 30 by the Department.

Title.Emphasis: Publication of certain information and advice

P1group.Title: Publication of certain information and advice

P2para.Text: The Department may arrange for the publication, in such form and in such manner as it considers appropriate, of such information relating to any matter which is connected with the carrying out by a company holding an appointment under Chapter I of Part III of the functions of a relevant undertaker as it may appear to the Department to be in the public interest to publish.

DOE may arrange for the publication, in such form and in such manner as it considers appropriate, of such information relating to any matter which is connected with its functions relating to the supply of water for domestic or food production purposes as it may appear to DOE to be in the public interest to publish.

The Authority may arrange for the publication, in such form and in such manner as it considers appropriate, of such information and advice as it may appear to the Authority to be expedient to give to any customer or potential customer of a company holding an appointment under Chapter I of Part III.
In arranging for the publication of any such information or advice the Department, DOE or the Authority shall have regard to the need for excluding, so far as that is practicable—

P3para.Text: any matter which relates to the affairs of an individual, where the publication of that matter would or might, in the opinion of the Department, DOE or (as the case may be) the Authority, seriously and prejudicially affect the interests of that individual; and

any matter which relates specifically to the affairs of a particular body of persons, whether corporate or unincorporate, where publication of that matter would or might, in the opinion of the Department, DOE or (as the case may be) the Authority, seriously and prejudicially affect the interests of that body.

P2para.Text: The CMA shall consult the Authority before publishing under section 6 of the Enterprise Act any information or advice which may be published by the Authority under paragraph (3).
The Department may—

P3para.Text: undertake, or contribute towards the cost of, investigations and research relevant to water supplies, sewerage and sewerage treatment; and
arrange for the publication of information on those matters.

P2para.Text: DOE may undertake, or contribute towards the cost of, investigations and research relevant to its functions relating to the supply of water for domestic or food production purposes

Text.Substitution: References in this Article to the functions of DOE relating to the supply of water for domestic or food production purposes are to its functions under Articles 107 to 112 and 118 to 125.

Title.Emphasis: Powers to acquire and duties to provide information

P1group.Title: Duties of undertakers to provide the Department with information

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of a company holding an appointment as a relevant undertaker to provide the Department with all such information relating to any matter which—

P3para.Text: is connected with, or with any proposals relating to, the carrying out by that company of the functions of a relevant undertaker; or
is material to the carrying out by the Department of any of its functions under this Order,

P2para.Text: as the Department may reasonably require.
Information required under this Article shall be provided in such form and manner, and be accompanied or supplemented by such explanations, as the Department may reasonably require.

The information which a company may be required to provide under this Article shall include information which, although it is not in the possession of that company or would not otherwise come into the possession of that company, is information which it is reasonable to require that company to obtain.
A requirement for the purposes of this Article shall be contained in a direction which—

P3para.Text: may describe the information to be provided in such manner as the Department considers appropriate;
may require the information to be provided on a particular occasion, in particular circumstances or from time to time; and
may be given to a particular company, to companies of a particular description or to all the companies holding appointments under Chapter I of Part II.

P2para.Text: The obligations of a relevant undertaker under this Article pursuant to a requirement under paragraph (1) shall be enforceable under Article 30 by the Department.

P1group.Title: Power to require information for enforcement purposes

P2para.Text: Where it appears to an enforcement authority that a company which holds an appointment as a relevant undertaker may be contravening, or may have contravened—

P3para.Text: any condition of the company's appointment in relation to which it is the relevant enforcement authority; or
any statutory or other requirement enforceable under Article 30 in relation to which it is the relevant enforcement authority,

P2para.Text: it may, for any purpose connected with such of its powers under Chapter II of Part III as are exercisable in relation to that matter, serve a notice under paragraph (2) on any person.
A notice under this paragraph is a notice—

P3para.Text: requiring the person on whom it is served to produce, at a time and place specified in the notice, to—

P4para.Text: the enforcing authority; or
any person appointed by the enforcing authority for the purpose,

P3para.Text: any documents which are specified or described in the notice and are in that person's custody or under his control; or

requiring that person, if he is carrying on a business, to provide, at the time and place and in the form and manner specified in the notice, the enforcing authority with such information as may be specified or described in the notice.

P2para.Text: No person shall be required under this Article to produce any documents which he could not be compelled to produce in civil proceedings in the High Court or, in complying with any requirement to provide information, to give any information which he could not be compelled to give in evidence in any such proceedings.

A person who, without reasonable excuse, fails to do anything required of him by a notice under paragraph (2) shall be guilty of an offence and liable, on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding level 5 on the standard scale.
A person who intentionally alters, suppresses or destroys any document which he has been required by any notice under paragraph (2) to produce shall be guilty of an offence and liable—

P3para.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding the statutory maximum;
on conviction on indictment, to a fine.

P2para.Text: If a person makes default in complying with a notice under paragraph (2), the High Court may, on the application of the enforcing authority which served the notice, make such order as the Court thinks fit for requiring the default to be made good; and any such order may provide that all the costs or expenses of and incidental to the application shall be borne by the person in default or by any officers of a company or other body who are responsible for its default.

Nothing in this Article shall be construed as restricting any power of the Department or the Authority under Article 260 or under the conditions of an appointment under Chapter I of Part III to require a company holding such an appointment to produce any document to it or to provide it with any information.
Expressions used in this Article and in Chapter II of Part III have the same meanings in this Article as in that Chapter.

P1group.Title: Provision of information to sewerage undertakers with respect to trade effluent discharges

P2para.Text: The owner or occupier of any land on or under which is situated any sewer, drain, pipe, channel or outlet used or intended to be used for discharging any trade effluent into a sewer of a sewerage undertaker shall, when requested to do so by the undertaker—

P3para.Text: produce to the undertaker all such plans of the sewer, drain, pipe, channel or outlet as the owner or, as the case may be, occupier possesses or is able without expense to obtain;
allow copies of the plans so produced by him to be made by, or under the directions of, the undertaker; and
provide to the undertaker all such information as the owner or, as the case may be, occupier can reasonably be expected to supply with respect to the sewer, drain, pipe, channel or outlet.

P2para.Text: A request by a sewerage undertaker for the purposes of this Article shall be made in writing.
Any person who fails to comply with this Article shall be guilty of an offence and liable, on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 3 on the standard scale.

Expressions used in this Article and in Chapter III of Part VI have the same meanings in this Article as in that Chapter; and, accordingly, Article 198 shall have effect for the purposes of this Article as it has effect for the purposes of that Chapter.

P1group.Title: Exchange of metering information between undertakers

P2para.Text: Where—

P3para.Text: different services are provided in relation to the same premises by different relevant undertakers;
one of those undertakers has obtained a reading from a meter used in determining the amount of any charges fixed in relation to those premises;
the charges in relation to those premises of another of those undertakers are fixed by reference to any matter to which the reading is relevant; and
that other undertaker has agreed to bear a reasonable proportion of the expenses of obtaining the reading together with the reasonable expenses of the disclosure of the reading to it,

P2para.Text: it shall be the duty of the undertaker who obtained the reading to disclose the reading to the other undertaker.
Any dispute between a relevant undertaker and any other person (including another such undertaker)—

P3para.Text: as to the terms to be contained in any agreement for the purposes of paragraph (1)(d); or
as to the amount of any expenses to be borne by any person under any such agreement,

P2para.Text: shall be referred to the arbitration of a single arbitrator appointed by agreement between the undertaker and that person or, in default of agreement, by the Authority.
The duties of a relevant undertaker under this Article shall be enforceable under Article 30 by the Authority.

P1group.Title: Information sharing for purposes of special provision in charges scheme

P2para.Text: This Article applies to any information relating to a consumer which is held by the Department of Finance and Personnel or the Northern Ireland Housing Executive for the purposes of the administration of—

P3para.Text: a scheme under Article 30A of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977; or
housing benefit.

P2para.Text: Information to which this Article applies must, if an authorised officer of a relevant undertaker so requires, be supplied to—

P3para.Text: the undertaker; or
any person or body providing services to the undertaker,

P2para.Text: for the purpose of enabling or assisting the undertaker to give effect to any special provision included in a charges scheme of the undertaker in compliance with regulations under Article 202(2)(d)(i).
Any requirement under paragraph (2) must specify—

P3para.Text: the description of information which is to be supplied;
in the case of information to be supplied to a person other than the authorised officer, the name and address of that person;
the form in which the information is to be supplied; and
the date by which the information is to be supplied.

P2para.Text: This Article—

P3para.Text: does not limit the circumstances in which information may be supplied apart from this Article; but
has effect despite any restriction on the purposes for which information may be disclosed or used.

P2para.Text: The Department may by order amend paragraph (1) by adding any description of information to, or removing any description of information from, the list in that paragraph; but no description of information may be added to that list unless it is information of a description held by a Northern Ireland department or the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.
In this Article—

Para.Text: “authorised officer”, in relation to a relevant undertaker, means an officer of the undertaker authorised for the purposes of this Article by the undertaker;
“consumer” has the same meaning as in Chapter I of Part VII;
“housing benefit” means housing benefit provided by virtue of a scheme under section 122 of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (c. 7).

P1group.Title: Restriction on disclosure of information

P2para.Text: Subject to the following provisions of this Article, no information with respect to any particular business which—

P3para.Text: has been obtained by virtue of any of the provisions of this Order or the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1973 (NI 2); and
relates to the affairs of any individual or to any particular business,

P2para.Text: shall, during the lifetime of that individual or so long as that business continues to be carried on, be disclosed without the consent of that individual or the person for the time being carrying on that business.
No person shall disclose any information provided to him under Article 262 or under Chapter III of Part VI except—

P3para.Text: with the consent of the person by whom the information was furnished;
in connection with the execution of that Chapter;
for the purposes of any proceedings arising under that Chapter (including any appeal, application to the Department, DOE or the Authority or an arbitration);
for the purposes of any criminal proceedings (whether or not so arising); or
for the purposes of any report of any proceedings falling within sub-paragraph (c) or (d).

P2para.Text: Paragraph (1) does not apply to any disclosure of information which is made—

P3para.Text: for the purpose of facilitating the performance by the Department, DOE, the Authority, the Council, the CMA or a district council of any functions under this Order, the Water Order, Part III of the Waste and Contaminated Land (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (NI 19) or regulations under Article 4 of the Environment (Northern Ireland) Order 2002 (NI 7);
for the purpose of facilitating the performance by a relevant undertaker of any of the duties imposed on it by or under this Order or the Water Order;
in pursuance of any duty imposed by Article 52;
for the purpose of facilitating the performance by a person or body mentioned in paragraph (4) of any functions under a statutory provision specified in paragraph (5);

for the purpose of facilitating the exercise by the Secretary of State or the Treasury of any powers conferred by the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (c. 8) or by any statutory provision relating to companies, insurance companies or insolvency;
for the purpose of facilitating the performance by any inspector appointed under the statutory provisions relating to companies of his functions;

for the purpose of facilitating the performance by the Department of Enterprise, Trade and Investment or DFP of any functions conferred on it by any statutory provision relating to companies or insolvency;

for the purpose of facilitating the performance by the official receiver for Northern Ireland of his functions under the statutory provisions relating to insolvency or for the purpose of facilitating the performance by a recognised professional body for the purposes of Article 350 of the Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 (NI 19) of its functions as such a body;
for the purpose of facilitating the performance by the Health and Safety Executive for Northern Ireland of any of its functions;
for the purpose of facilitating the performance by the Comptroller and Auditor General for Northern Ireland of any of his functions;
for the purpose of facilitating the performance by the Appeals Commission of any of its functions;

Text.Addition: for the purpose of facilitating the performance by the lead enforcement authority (as defined in section 33(1) of the Estate Agents Act 1979) of any of its functions under the Estate Agents Act 1979;

P3para.Text: in connection with the investigation of any criminal offence or for the purposes of any criminal proceedings;

for the purposes of any civil proceedings brought under or by virtue of this Order or the Water Order or any statutory provision specified in paragraph (5), or of any arbitration under this Order or the Water Order; or
in pursuance of an assimilated obligation,

P2para.Text: and sections 17 and 18 of the Anti-terrorism, Crime and Security Act 2001 (c. 24) apply to this paragraph as if it were listed in Schedule 4 to that Act.
The persons and bodies specified for the purposes of paragraph (3)(d) are—

P3para.Text: a Minister of the Crown;
a Northern Ireland department;
the CMA;
the Office of Communications;
the Authority;
the Civil Aviation Authority;

Text.Substitution: the Financial Conduct Authority;
the Prudential Regulation Authority.

P2para.Text: The statutory provisions specified for the purposes of paragraphs (3)(d) and (m) are—

P3para.Text: the Trade Descriptions Act 1968 (c. 29);
the Fair Trading Act 1973 (c. 41);
the Consumer Credit Act 1974 (c. 39);
the Estate Agents Act 1979 (c. 38);
the Competition Act 1980 (c. 2);
the Telecommunications Act 1984 (c. 12);
the Consumer Protection (Northern Ireland) Order 1987 (NI 20);
the Electricity (Northern Ireland) Order 1992 (NI 1);
Part IV of the Airports (Northern Ireland) Order 1994 (NI 1);
the Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (NI 2);
the Competition Act 1998 (c. 41);
Part I of the Transport Act 2000 (c 38);
the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (c. 8);
the Enterprise Act;
the Communications Act 2003 (c. 21);
the Energy (Northern Ireland) Order 2003 (NI 6).

Text.Addition: the Business Protection from Misleading Marketing Regulations 2008;
Parts 3 and 4 of the Enterprise and Regulatory Reform Act 2013.
the following provisions of the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act 2024—
Part 3;
Part 4;
Chapter 2 of Part 5.

P2para.Text: The Department may by order modify paragraph (3), (4) or (5).
Nothing in paragraph (1) shall be construed—

P3para.Text: as limiting the matters which may be published under Article 67, 151 or 259 or may be included in, or made public as part of, a report of the Department, DOE, the Authority, the Council, a district council or the CMA under any provision of this Order, the Water Order, Part III of the Waste and Contaminated Land (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (NI 19) or regulations under Article 4 of the Environment (Northern Ireland) Order 2002 (NI 7), or
as applying to any information which has been so published or has been made public as part of such a report or to any information exclusively of a statistical nature.

P2para.Text: Subject to paragraph (9), nothing in paragraph (1) shall preclude the disclosure of information—

P3para.Text: if the disclosure is of information relating to a matter connected with the carrying out of the functions of a relevant undertaker and is made by one Minister of the Crown or government department to another; or

if the disclosure is for the purpose of enabling or assisting any public or other authority for the time being designated for the purposes of this Article by an order made by the Department to discharge any functions which are specified in the order.

P2para.Text: An order under paragraph (8) shall be subject to negative resolution; and where such an order designates an authority for the purposes of sub-paragraph (b) of that paragraph, the order may—

P3para.Text: impose conditions subject to which the disclosure of information is permitted by virtue of that paragraph; and
otherwise restrict the circumstances in which disclosure is so permitted.

P2para.Text: Any person who discloses any information in contravention of the preceding provisions of this Article shall be guilty of an offence.
A person who is guilty of an offence under this Article by virtue of paragraph (1) shall be liable—

P3para.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding the statutory maximum;
on conviction on indictment, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or to a fine or to both.

P2para.Text: A person who is guilty of an offence under this Article by virtue of paragraph (2) shall be liable, on summary conviction, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months or to a fine not exceeding level 3 on the standard scale or to both.

Information obtained by the Authority in the exercise of functions which are exercisable concurrently with the CMA under Part I of the Competition Act 1998 (c. 41) is subject to Part 9 of the Enterprise Act (information) and not to the preceding provisions of this Article.

P1group.Title: Unauthorised disclosure of information relating to particular persons

P2para.Text: A person to whom this Article applies commits an offence if he discloses without lawful authority any information(

P3para.Text: which he acquired in the course of his employment;
which is, or is derived from, information supplied under—

P4para.Text: Article 264; or
Article 3 of the Water and Sewerage Services (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Northern Ireland) Order 2006; and

P3para.Text: which relates to a particular person.

P2para.Text: This Article applies to any person who is employed—

P3para.Text: by a relevant undertaker; or
in the provision of services to a relevant undertaker for the purposes of any arrangements mentioned in Article 264(2);

P2para.Text: and “employment” in relation to any such person shall be construed accordingly.
It is not an offence under this Article to disclose information which has previously been disclosed to the public with lawful authority.
It is a defence for a person charged with an offence under this Article to show that at the time of the alleged offence—

P3para.Text: he believed that he was making the disclosure in question with lawful authority and had no reasonable cause to believe otherwise; or
he believed that the information in question had previously been disclosed to the public with lawful authority and had no reasonable cause to believe otherwise.

P2para.Text: A person who is guilty of an offence under this Article shall be liable—

P3para.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding the statutory maximum;
on conviction on indictment, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or to a fine or to both.

P2para.Text: For the purposes of this Article a disclosure of information is to be regarded as made with lawful authority if, and only if, it is made—

P3para.Text: with the authority of a relevant undertaker for the purposes of the exercise of its functions;
in accordance with any statutory provision or order of a court;
for the purposes of any criminal proceedings; or
with the consent of the person to whom the information relates.

P1group.Title: Provision of false information

P2para.Text: If any person, in providing any information or making any application under or for the purposes of any provision of this Order, makes any statement which he knows to be false in a material particular, or recklessly makes any statement which is false in a material particular, he shall be guilty of an offence and liable—

P3para.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding the statutory maximum;
on conviction on indictment, to a fine.

P2para.Text: Proceedings for an offence under paragraph (1) shall not be instituted except by or with the consent of the relevant Department or the Director of Public Prosecutions for Northern Ireland.
In paragraph (2) “relevant Department” means—

P3para.Text: DOE, in the case of an offence alleged to have been committed in providing any information or making any application under or for the purposes of any provision in Articles 107 to 112 or in Articles 118 to 123;
the Department in any other case.

P1group.Title: Interpretation of this Part

P2para.Text: In this Part—

Text.Substitution: ” has the meaning given by section 2(1) of the Companies Act 2006;

Para.Text: “debentures” includes debenture stock;
“securities”, in relation to a company, includes shares, debentures, bonds and other securities of the company, whether or not constituting a charge on the assets of the company;
“shares” includes stock;

Text.Substitution: ” and “
” have the same meanings as in the Companies Acts (see sections 1159 and 1162 of the Companies Act 2006);

Para.Text: “the successor company” has the meaning assigned to it by Article 269(1);
“the transfer scheme” has the meaning assigned to it by Article 270(1).

P2para.Text: A company shall be regarded for the purposes of this Part as wholly owned by the Crown at any time when none of the issued shares in the company is held otherwise than—

P3para.Text: by, or by a nominee of, the Department; or
by a company which is itself wholly owned by the Crown.

P1group.Title: The successor company

P2para.Text: The Department may by order nominate as the successor company for the purposes of this Part a company which—

P3para.Text: is a company limited by shares (within the meaning of section 3 of the Companies Act 2006); and
is wholly owned by the Crown;

P2para.Text: and references in this Part to “the successor company” are references to the company so nominated.
The successor company shall not—

P3para.Text: be regarded as the servant or agent of the Crown;
have any status, immunity or privilege of the Crown,

P2para.Text: and its property shall not be regarded as property of, or held on behalf of, the Crown.

Title.Emphasis: Transfer to successor company of certain assets and liabilities of the Department

P1group.Title: Transfer scheme

P2para.Text: The Department shall make a scheme (“the transfer scheme”) for the transfer to the successor company of—

P3para.Text: specified water service assets and water service liabilities of the Department; and
such other assets and liabilities of the Department as may be specified.

P2para.Text: On such day as the Department may by order specify (the “transfer date”) the assets and liabilities specified for transfer to the successor company shall, by virtue of this paragraph, become assets and liabilities of that company.
The provisions of Schedule 11 shall apply in relation to—

P3para.Text: the transfer scheme; and
the transfer effected by paragraph (2);

P2para.Text: and that paragraph shall have effect subject to the provisions of that Schedule.
In this Article—

Para.Text: “specified” means specified in the transfer scheme;

“water service assets” and “water service liabilities” mean, respectively, the assets and liabilities which, either wholly or in part, subsist immediately before the transfer date for the purposes of, or in connection with, or are otherwise attributable to, the functions exercisable by the Department immediately before that date under the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1973 (NI 2).

P1group.Title: Initial Departmental holding in the successor company

P2para.Text: As a consequence of the vesting in the successor company of any assets and liabilities under Article 270, the company shall issue such securities of the company as the Department may direct to the Department.
The Department shall not give a direction under paragraph (1) at a time when the successor company has ceased to be wholly owned by the Crown.
Securities required to be issued under this Article shall be issued at such time or times and on such terms as the Department may direct.
Shares in the successor company which are issued under this Article—

P3para.Text: shall be of such nominal value as the Department may direct; and
shall be issued as fully paid and treated for the purposes of the Companies Acts as if they had been paid up by virtue of the payment to the company of their nominal value in cash.

P2para.Text: For the purposes of the Companies Acts . . . , the Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 (NI 19) and the Company Directors Disqualification (Northern Ireland) Order 2002 (NI 4), the Crown is not to be treated as a shadow director of the successor company by reason that the company is wholly owned by the Crown or that the directors of that company are accustomed to act in accordance with the Crown's directions or instructions.
Paragraph (5) applies to any subsidiary or subsidiary undertaking of the successor company as it applies to the successor company.

P1group.Title: Departmental investment in securities of the successor company

P1para.Text: The Department may at any time acquire—

P3para.Text: securities of the successor company; or
rights to subscribe for any such securities.

P1group.Title: Disposal of securities, etc

P2para.Text: The Department shall not dispose of any securities or rights issued under Article 271 or acquired under Article 272 except—

P3para.Text: in a case of a disposal requiring Assembly approval, with that approval; and
in the case of any other disposal, with the consent of DFP.

P2para.Text: A disposal requires Assembly approval if, in the opinion of the Department, the effect of the disposal would be that the successor company is no longer controlled by the Department.
Assembly approval is given to a disposal if the disposal is approved by a resolution of the Assembly passed on a resolution specifying—

P3para.Text: the size and nature of the disposal;
the person to whom it is to be made.

P2para.Text: For the purposes of this Article the successor company is controlled by the Department if the Department holds securities of the company entitling it to exercise or control the exercise of a majority of the voting rights in the company which are exercisable at a general meeting on all, or substantially all, matters.

P1group.Title: Exercise of Departmental powers through nominees

P2para.Text: The Department may, for the purposes of Article 271 or 272, appoint any person to act as the nominee, or one of the nominees, of the Department; and—

P3para.Text: securities of the successor company may be issued under Article 271 to any nominee of the Department appointed for the purposes of that Article; and
any such nominee appointed for the purposes of Article 272 may acquire securities or rights under that Article,

P2para.Text: in accordance with directions given by the Department.

Any person holding any securities or rights as a nominee of the Department under paragraph (1) shall hold and deal with them (or any of them) on such terms and in such manner as the Department may direct.
For the purposes of Article 273—

P3para.Text: any securities or rights held by a nominee of the Department shall be treated as held by the Department; and
any disposal by a nominee of the Department shall be treated as a disposal by the Department.

P1group.Title: Statutory reserves

P2para.Text: If the Department so directs at any time before the successor company ceases to be wholly owned by the Crown, such sum as may be specified in the direction shall be carried by the successor company to a reserve (“the statutory reserve”).
The statutory reserve may only be applied by the successor company—

P3para.Text: in paying up unissued shares of the company to be allotted to members of the company as fully paid bonus shares; or
if the Department so directs, as if it were profits available for distribution within the meaning of section 830(2) of the Companies Act 2006.

P2para.Text: Notwithstanding paragraph (2), the statutory reserve shall not count as an undistributable reserve of the successor company for the purposes of section 831(4)(d) of the Companies Act 2006; but for the purpose of determining under that section whether the successor company may make a distribution at any time any amount for the time being standing to the credit of the statutory reserve (excluding any amount which by virtue of paragraph (2)(b) is authorised to be, but has not yet been, applied as if it were profits available for distribution) shall be treated for the purposes of section 831(4)(c) of that Act as if it were unrealised profits of the company.

P1group.Title: Accounts, etc. of successor company

Text.Substitution: The Department shall, as soon as practicable, lay before the Assembly a copy of the documents mentioned in paragraph (2) which are—
if the successor company is a private company, sent to members in accordance with section 423 of the Companies Act 2006, or
if the successor company is a public company, laid before the company in general meeting in accordance with section 437 of that Act,
at a time when the company is wholly owned by the Crown.

P2para.Text: The documents referred to in paragraph (1) are—

P3para.Text: the profit and loss account;
the group accounts;
the balance sheet;
the auditors' report;
the directors' report;

P2para.Text: and expressions used in this paragraph have the same meaning as in the Companies Act 2006.

For the purposes of any statutory accounts of the successor company, the vesting in the successor company under Article 270 of assets and liabilities of the Department shall be taken to have been effected immediately after the end of the last complete financial year of the Department to end on or before the transfer date and to have been a vesting of such of the assets and liabilities to which the Department was entitled or subject immediately before the end of the year as are specified in the transfer scheme.
The value of any asset and the amount of any liability which is taken under paragraph (3) to have been vested in the successor company shall be taken to have been—

P3para.Text: the value or amount determined by or under the transfer scheme, or

(if no such provision is included in the transfer scheme) the value or amount assigned to the asset or liability for the purposes of the corresponding statement of accounts prepared by the Department in respect of its last complete financial year to end on or before the transfer date.

P2para.Text: In this Article “statutory accounts” means any accounts (including group accounts) prepared for the purpose of any provision of the Companies Acts . . . .

P1group.Title: Restrictions on borrowing, charging, etc.

P2para.Text: If articles of association of the successor company confer on the Department powers exercisable with the consent of DFP for, or in connection with, restricting the sums of money which may be borrowed or raised by the successor company during any period, those powers shall be exercisable in the public interest notwithstanding any rule of law or any statutory provision.
For the purposes of this Article an alteration of the articles of association of the successor company shall be disregarded if the alteration—

P3para.Text: has the effect of conferring or extending any such power as is mentioned in paragraph (1); and
is made at a time when that company has ceased to be wholly owned by the Crown.

P2para.Text: The successor company may not, at any time before it ceases to be wholly owned by the Crown, charge any of its assets without the approval of the Department and DFP.

P1group.Title: Departmental lending, guarantees, etc.

P2para.Text: The Department may give financial assistance to the successor company in such form and upon such terms and conditions as the Department may determine.
Without prejudice to the generality of paragraph (1), the Department may in exercise of its powers under that paragraph—

P3para.Text: make loans or grants to the successor company;
write off the principal and interest of any loans so made or enter into an arrangement for the conversion into share capital of the principal and interest of loans so made;
guarantee obligations (arising out of loans or otherwise) incurred by the successor company.

P2para.Text: The Department may, upon such terms and conditions as it may determine, give to any person entering into a contract—

P3para.Text: with the successor company; or
which in the opinion of the Department is directly or indirectly related to a contract entered or to be entered into by another person with the successor company,

P2para.Text: any form of guarantee to meet any contingency arising out of or in connection with the contract.

Immediately after any guarantee is given under this Article, the Department shall lay a statement of the guarantee before the Assembly; and immediately after any sum is issued for fulfilling a guarantee so given, the Department shall so lay a statement relating to that sum.
If any sums are issued in fulfillment of a guarantee given under this Article the successor company shall make to the Department, at such times and in such manner as the Department may direct—

P3para.Text: payments of such amounts as the Department may direct in or towards repayment of the sums so issued; and
payments of interest on what is outstanding for the time being in respect of sums so issued at such rate as the Department may direct.

P2para.Text: In this Article “guarantee” includes any form of indemnity or assurance.
This Article applies to any subsidiary or subsidiary undertaking of the successor company as it applies to the successor company

P1group.Title: Exercise of certain functions of the Department under this Part

P1para.Text: The Department shall not, except with the consent of DFP, exercise any power under any of the following provisions of this Part, namely—

P3para.Text: Article 269(1);
Article 270(1);
Article 271(1) to (4);
Article 272;
Article 274;
Article 275(1);
Article 278(1) to (3) and (7);
paragraph 3 of Schedule 11.

P1group.Title: Pollution of water

P1para.Text: For Article 7 of the Water Order substitute—

P1group.Title: Pollution of water: offences

P2para.Text: Subject to the following provisions of this Part, a person commits an offence if, whether knowingly or otherwise—

P3para.Text: he discharges or deposits any poisonous, noxious or polluting matter so that it enters a waterway or water contained in any underground strata; or

he discharges or deposits any matter so that it enters a waterway or water contained in any underground strata and tends either directly or in combination with similar acts (whether his own or those of another) to impede the proper flow of the water of the waterway or strata in a manner leading or likely to lead to pollution or a substantial aggravation of pollution due to other causes or of its consequences.

P2para.Text: Subject to the following provisions of this Part, a person commits an offence if, by any means whatsoever, he makes any discharge of any trade or sewage effluent—

P3para.Text: into a waterway or water contained in any underground strata; or
from land, through a pipe, into the sea outside the seaward limits of any waterway.

P2para.Text: On a person's conviction of an offence under paragraph (1), the court by or before which he is convicted may on the application of the Department, of which not less than 10 days' notice has been given to the person charged, make an order directing him to take such measures as the court may consider necessary to remedy or nullify any contravention of paragraph (1).
A person who fails to comply with an order under paragraph (3) shall be guilty of an offence.
In paragraph (1) any reference to matter entering a waterway or water contained in any underground strata includes a reference to its entering the waterway or water by being carried into it.
Subject to the following provisions of this Part, a person who contravenes the conditions of any consent under Article 7A(3)(a) shall be guilty of an offence.
A person guilty of an offence under this Article shall be liable—

P3para.Text: on conviction on indictment, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years or to a fine or to both;
on summary conviction, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 months or to a fine not exceeding £20,000 or to both.

P1group.Title: Exceptions

P2para.Text: A person shall not be guilty of an offence under Article 7(2) or (6) by reason of any discharge of trade or sewage effluent from a vessel.
A person shall not be guilty of an offence under Article 7(1), (2) or (6) in respect of the discharge or deposit of any effluent or other matter if—

P3para.Text: the discharge or deposit is made in an emergency in order to avoid danger to life or health;
that person takes all such steps as are reasonably practicable in the circumstances for minimising the extent of the discharge or deposit and of its polluting effects; and
particulars of the discharge or deposit are furnished to the Department as soon as reasonably practicable after it occurs.

P2para.Text: A person shall not be guilty of an offence under Article 7(1) or (2) or (6) in respect of the discharge or deposit of any effluent or other matter if the discharge or deposit is made under and in accordance with, or as a result of any act or omission under and in accordance with—

P3para.Text: a consent given by the Department under this Article;
a disposal licence under Article 7 of the Pollution Control and Local Government (Northern Ireland) Order 1978;
a licence granted under Part II of the Food and Environment Protection Act 1985;
an authorisation granted under the Industrial Pollution Control (Northern Ireland) Order 1997;
a waste management licence granted under the Waste and Contaminated Land (Northern Ireland) Order 1997;
a permit granted under regulations under Article 4 of the Environment (Northern Ireland) Order 2002;
Article 226 of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006;
any statutory order which expressly confers power to discharge effluent into water;
any prescribed statutory provision.

P2para.Text: Schedule 1 shall have effect, subject to Article 13, with respect to the making of applications for consents under paragraph (3)(a) (“discharge consents”) and with respect to the giving, revocation, transfer and modification of such consents.
Nothing in any disposal licence shall be treated for the purposes of paragraph (3) as authorising—

P3para.Text: any discharge as is mentioned in Article 7(2); or
any act or omission so far as it results in any such discharge.

P2para.Text: In paragraph (3)(h) “statutory order” means—

P3para.Text: an order under Article 138(1)(c) or 228 of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006;
an order under Article 23(2);
any other order or scheme made under a statutory provision.

P2para.Text: A person shall not be guilty of an offence under Article 7(1), (2) or (6) if he discharges any effluent or other matter into a drain mentioned in paragraph (8) so that it enters a waterway or water contained in underground strata, if the Department for Regional Development has given its consent to the discharge (either unconditionally or subject to conditions which were observed).
The drains referred to in paragraph (7) are —

P3para.Text: a road drain within the meaning of Article 45 of the Roads (Northern Ireland) Order 1993; or
a drain constructed and laid by the Department for Regional Development under Article 45(1)(b) of that Order.

P1group.Title: Discharges into and from public sewers, etc.

P2para.Text: A person shall not be guilty of an offence under Article 7(1), (2) or (6) in respect of a discharge made into a sewer or waste water treatment works vested in a sewerage undertaker if the undertaker was bound to receive the discharge there either unconditionally or subject to conditions which were observed.

A sewerage undertaker shall not be guilty of an offence under Article 7(1), (2) or (6) by reason only of the fact that a discharge from a sewer or works vested in the undertaker contravenes conditions of a consent relating to the discharge if—

P3para.Text: the contravention is attributable to a discharge which another person made into the sewer or works;
the undertaker either was not bound to receive the discharge into the sewer or works or was bound to receive it there subject to conditions which were not observed; and
the undertaker could not reasonably have been expected to prevent the discharge into the sewer or works.

P2para.Text: Paragraph (4) applies for the purpose of determining liability where—

P3para.Text: sewage effluent is discharged as mentioned in Article 7(1) or(2) from any sewer or works (“the discharging sewer”) vested in a sewerage undertaker (“the discharging undertaker”); and

the effluent was, before being discharged from the discharging sewer, discharged through a main connection into that sewer or into any other sewer or waste water treatment works vested in the discharging undertaker by another sewerage undertaker (“the sending undertaker”) under an agreement having effect between the discharging undertaker and the sending undertaker under Article 167 of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006.

P2para.Text: The sending undertaker shall be deemed to have made the discharge if—

P3para.Text: matter included in the discharge was received by it into a sewer or works vested in it; and
it was bound (either unconditionally or subject to conditions which were observed) to receive that matter into that sewer or waste water treatment works.

P2para.Text: The Department for Regional Development may by regulations make further provision for the purpose of determining liability under Article 7(1), (2) or (6) as between two or more persons in respect of any discharge into or from any public sewer or any waste water treatment works.
In this Article “main connection” has the same meaning as in Article 167 of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006.

P1group.Title: Relationship of Part II of Water Order with other statutory provisions

P2para.Text: For Article 10 of the Water Order (relationship with other statutory provisions) substitute—

P1para.Text: Relationship of this Part with other statutory provisions controlling pollution

The discharge or deposit of any effluent or matter made under and in accordance with, or as a result of any act or omission under and in accordance with, a discharge consent shall not constitute an offence under or a contravention of—

P3para.Text: section 41 of the Foyle Fisheries Act (Northern Ireland) 1952 or any regulations under section 13 of that Act;
section 47 of the Fisheries Act (Northern Ireland) 1966 or any byelaws under section 26 of that Act;
regulations under Article 4 of the Environment (Northern Ireland) Order 2002;
Article 23 of the Industrial Pollution Control (Northern Ireland) Order 1997; or
any regulations under Article 14 or 36.

P1group.Title: Charges for transfer of discharge consent

P1para.Text: In Article 11 of the Water Order (scheme of charges) in paragraph (1)(a) after “variation” insert “ or transfer ”.

P1group.Title: Anti-pollution works

P2para.Text: In Article 16 of the Water Order (anti-pollution works) in paragraph (1)(b)(iii) for “in them” substitute “ dependent on the aquatic environment of the waters ”.
In Article 17 of that Order (notices to carry out anti-pollution works) in paragraph (2)(b)(iii) for “in them” substitute “ dependent on the aquatic environment of the waters ”.

P1group.Title: Provision of information

P1para.Text: In the Water Order after Article 28 insert—

P1group.Title: Provision of information

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of the Department—

P3para.Text: to provide a water undertaker with all such information to which this Article applies as is in the possession of the Department and is reasonably requested by the undertaker for purposes connected with the carrying out of its functions; and
to provide reasonable facilities to all persons—

P4para.Text: for the inspection of the contents of any records kept by the Department and containing information to which this Article applies; and
for the taking of copies of, or of extracts from, any such records.

P2para.Text: It shall be the duty of every water undertaker to provide the Department with all such information to which this Article applies as is in the possession of the undertaker and is reasonably requested by the Department for purposes connected with the carrying out of any of its functions.
Where records of the flow, level or volume of water in any waterway are kept by a person other than a water undertaker, the Department shall have the right at all reasonable times—

P3para.Text: to inspect the contents of any of those records; and
to take copies of, or of extracts from, the contents of any of those records,

P2para.Text: and any person who, without reasonable excuse, refuses or fails to permit the Department to exercise its right under this paragraph shall be guilty of an offence and liable, on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding level 1 on the standard scale.

Information provided to a water undertaker or to the Department under paragraph (1) or (2) shall be provided in such form and in such manner and at such times as the undertaker or, as the case may be, the Department may reasonably require; and the duties of the Department under paragraph (1) shall extend to information provided to or obtained by the Department under paragraph (2) or (3).
Information or facilities provided under paragraph (1) or (2) to—

P3para.Text: the Department,
the Department of Agriculture and Rural Development;
the Department of Culture, Arts and Leisure;
a water undertaker; or
Waterways Ireland,

P2para.Text: shall be provided free of charge; and facilities provided under paragraph (1) to other persons may be provided on terms requiring the payment by persons making use of the facilities of such reasonable charges as the Department may determine.

The duties of a water undertaker under paragraph (2) shall be enforceable under Article 30 of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006 by the Northern Ireland Authority for Utility Regulation.
This Article applies to information about—

P3para.Text: the flow, level or volume of any water in any waterway or contained in underground strata;
rainfall or any fall of snow, hail or sleet; or
the evaporation of any water.

P1group.Title: Restriction on disclosure of information

P1para.Text: After Article 29 of the Water Order insert—

P1group.Title: Restriction on disclosure of information

P2para.Text: Subject to the following provisions of this Article, no information with respect to any particular business which—

P3para.Text: has been obtained by virtue of any of the provisions of this Order; and
relates to the affairs of any individual or to any particular business,

P2para.Text: shall, during the lifetime of that individual or so long as that business continues to be carried on, be disclosed without the consent of that individual or the person for the time being carrying on that business.
Paragraph (1) does not apply to any disclosure of information which is made—

P3para.Text: for the purpose of facilitating the performance by the Department, the Department for Regional Development, the Northern Ireland Authority for Utility Regulation, the General Consumer Council, the Competition Commission or the Appeals Commission of any functions under this Order or the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006;
for the purpose of facilitating the performance by a relevant undertaker of any of the duties imposed on it by or under this Order or the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006;
for the purposes of a register maintained under Article 30.

P1group.Title: Civil liability

P1para.Text: After Article 36 of the Water Order insert—

P1group.Title: Civil Liability

P1para.Text: Except in so far as this Part expressly otherwise provides and subject to the provisions of section 20(1) of the Interpretation Act (Northern Ireland) 1954 (c. 33) (which relates to offences under two or more laws), nothing in this Part—

P3para.Text: confers a right of action in any civil proceedings (other than proceedings for the recovery of a fine) in respect of any contravention of this Part or any subordinate legislation, consent or other instrument made, given or issued under this Part;
derogates from any right of action or other remedy (whether civil or criminal) in proceedings instituted otherwise than under this Part; or
affects any restriction imposed by or under any other statutory provision.

P1group.Title: Liability to prosecution for certain offences

P1para.Text: Renumber Article 37 of the Water Order (prosecutions) as paragraph (1) of that Article and after that paragraph insert—

P2para.Text: Without prejudice to section 20(2) of the Interpretation Act (Northern Ireland) 1954 (c. 33), where the commission by any person of an offence under any provision of this Part (except Article 26(6), 28(3) or 29) is due to the act or default of some other person, that other person may be charged with and convicted of the offence whether or not proceedings for the offence are taken against the first-mentioned person.

P1group.Title: Application of Part II of the Water Order to the Crown

P2para.Text: For Article 38A of the Water Order insert—

P1group.Title: Application of this Part to the Crown

P2para.Text: Subject to the provisions of this Article, this Part binds the Crown.

No contravention by the Crown of any provision made by or under this Part shall make the Crown criminally liable; but the High Court may, on the application of the Department, declare unlawful any act or omission of the Crown which constitutes such a contravention.
Notwithstanding anything in paragraph (2), any provision made by or under this Part shall apply to persons in the public service of the Crown as it applies to other persons.

If the Secretary of State certifies that it appears to him, as respects any Crown land and any powers of entry exercisable in relation to it specified in the certificate, that it is requisite or expedient that, in the interests of national security, the powers should not be exercisable in relation to that land, those powers shall not be exercised in relation to that land.
Subject to paragraph (4), the powers conferred by Article 24 shall be exercisable in relation to Crown land only with the consent of the appropriate authority.
In this Article any reference to the Crown includes a reference to the Crown in right of Her Majesty's Government in Northern Ireland.
In this Article—

Para.Text: “the appropriate authority” in relation to any land, means—
in the case of land belonging to Her Majesty in right of the Crown and forming part of the Crown Estate, the Crown Estate Commissioners;
in the case of any other land belonging to Her Majesty in right of the Crown, the government department having the management of that land;
in the case of land belonging to a government department or held in trust for Her Majesty for the purposes of a government department, that government department;

and, if any question arises as to what authority is the appropriate authority in relation to any land, that question shall be referred to the Department of Finance and Personnel or, as the case may be, the Treasury, whose decision shall be final;
“Crown estate” means an estate—
belonging to Her Majesty in right of the Crown; or
belonging to a government department or held in trust for Her Majesty for the purposes of a government department;
“Crown land” means land in which there is a Crown estate.

P1group.Title: Matters to be taken into account in exercising certain functions under Part III of Water Order

P1para.Text: In Article 40(1) of the Water Order (matters to be taken into account in exercising functions under Part III of that Order) after sub-paragraph (e) insert—

P3para.Text: the duties imposed under Parts III to VI of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006 on any relevant undertaker (within the meaning of that Order) which is likely to be affected by the exercise of the function in question;

P1group.Title: Discharge consents

P2para.Text: Schedule 1 to the Water Order (discharge consents) is amended as follows.
After paragraph 1 insert—

P1group.Title: Consultation in connection with applications

P2para.Text: Subject to sub-paragraph (2), the Department shall give notice of any application under paragraph 1, together with a copy of the application, to the persons who are prescribed to be consulted under this paragraph and shall do so within the specified period for notification.

The Department may by regulations exempt any class of application from the requirements of this paragraph or exclude any class of information contained in applications from those requirements, in all cases or as respects specified classes only of persons to be consulted.
Any representations made by the persons so consulted within the period allowed shall be considered by the Department in determining the application.
For the purposes of sub-paragraph (1)—

P3para.Text: persons are prescribed to be consulted on any description of application if they are persons specified for the purposes of applications of that description in regulations made by the Department; and
the “specified period for notification” is the period specified in the regulations.

P2para.Text: Any representations made by any other persons within the period allowed shall also be considered by the Department in determining the application.
Subject to sub-paragraph (7), the period allowed for making representations is—

P3para.Text: in the case of persons prescribed to be consulted, the period of 6 weeks beginning with the date on which notice of the application was given under sub-paragraph (1), and
in the case of other persons, the period of 6 weeks beginning with the date on which the making of the application was advertised in pursuance of paragraph 1(1)(b).

P2para.Text: The Department may, by regulations, substitute for any period for the time being specified in sub-paragraph (6)(a) or (b), such other period as it considers appropriate.
In paragraph 2(1)(b) at the end add “ or paragraph 1A ”.
In paragraph 4(1)(a) for “Article 9” substitute “ Article 7(2) ”.
In paragraph 8 omit—

P3para.Text: in sub-paragraphs (2) and (3), the words “, subject to sub-paragraph (4),”; and
sub-paragraph (4).

P2para.Text: In paragraph 8 for sub-paragraph (6) substitute—
Where a discharge consent given under paragraph 2 or 4 is to be transferred under sub-paragraph (1)—

P3para.Text: the person from whom and the person to whom the consent is to be transferred shall give joint notice to the Department of the proposed transfer;
the notice may specify the date on which it is proposed that the transfer should take effect;
within 21 days beginning with the date of receipt of the notice duly given in accordance with head (a), the Department shall—

P4para.Text: arrange to amend the consent by substituting the name of the transferee as holder of the consent; and
serve notice on the transferor and the transferee that the amendment has been made; and

P3para.Text: the transfer shall take effect from the later of—

P4para.Text: the date on which the Department amends the consent; and
the date (if any) specified in the joint notice under paragraph (a).

P2para.Text: A joint notice under sub-paragraph (6)(a) shall include such information as may be prescribed.

If the person from whom the consent is to be transferred is a person in whom the consent has vested by virtue of sub-paragraph (2) or (3), a joint notice given under sub-paragraph (6)(a) shall be of no effect unless the notice required by sub-paragraph (7) has been given.

A notice or other instrument given by or on behalf of the Department pursuant to sub-paragraph (6) shall not constitute an instrument signifying the consent of the Department for the purposes of paragraph 6.
In paragraph 8(9), omit the words “(6) or”.

P1group.Title: Minor amendments

P2para.Text: The Water Order is amended as follows.
In Article 2(2) for the definitions of “public sewage treatment works” and “public sewer” substitute—
“public sewer” has the same meaning as in the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006;
In Article 2(2) for the definitions of “sewage”, “sewage treatment works” “sewage effluent” and “sewerage undertaker” substitute—
“sewage effluent” includes any effluent from any waste water treatment works, but does not include surface water (within the meaning of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006);
In Article 2(2) after the definition of “vessel” insert—
